tox.ini for locally run automated tests with multiple python versions
issue_body:
Currently the package has definition of integration tests by means of  `.travis.yml`. This allows for automated testing after changes are committed, however, a developer cannot run such tests locally without travis service.

# Proposed solution (keep it simple)
- create `tox.ini` with definition of environments for all target python versions. Such environments would be run by default (would be listed in top section).
- each environment would run all the tests, which are easy to handle. Candidates for tests to skip are all tests requiring access to Internet or other installed services (like Mongo).
- `.travis.yml` would be kept as it is now as it runs more extensive set of tests.

# Possible extensions of proposed solution
- drive even extended tests (requiring access to Internet or using external services like Mongo) by tox.ini. Possibly allow skiping such tests, e.g. by means of some env. variables or similar methods.
- modify `.travis.yml` to use what extended `tox.ini` is providing (I have seen couple of such examples). This way automated and local tests could be the same (or very similar).

If such idea sounds acceptable, I could provide PR with initial Proposed solution, which would conclude this issue.

Extended solution may follow, but it could include more work and extend the basic functionality too far blocking possible simplification of locally run tests for developer.

issue_title: Feature Request: run() method in FlowerCommand
issue_body:
Celery's `worker` `Command` has a nice `run(...)` method ([here](https://github.com/celery/celery/blob/master/celery/bin/worker.py#L230)) that allows you to start a worker from Python, rather than the command.  This is nice for a number of reasons, we mainly use it for starting workers inside integration tests.  We are adding Flower to our program, and it would be nice to have a similar `run(...)` method.  This is in comparison to what I have put together for now (unless I'm missing something obvious in the code):
```
app = Celery(...)
flower = FlowerCommand(app)
flower.run_from_argv("flower", argv="--port={}".format(celery_port))
```

Would there be some interest in this?

issue_title: Use ~= for requirements

issue_title: Incorrect units on ureg.faraday?
issue_body:
The [Faraday constant](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Faraday_constant) is 96485.3329 coulombs per mole.

However, if I'm interpreting things correctly, the units in pint v0.8.1 appear to be wrong:

```
>>> print(float(ureg.faraday / ureg.coulomb))
96485.3399
>>> print(float(ureg.faraday / (ureg.coulomb / ureg.mole)))
---------------------------------------------------------------------------
DimensionalityError                       Traceback (most recent call last)
<ipython-input-43-0838b62a3617> in <module>()
...
DimensionalityError: Cannot convert from 'faraday * mole / coulomb' to 'dimensionless'
```

I can't speak to `ureg.chemical_faraday` or `ureg.physical_faraday`, but AFAICT [all three](https://github.com/hgrecco/pint/blob/697834d17d6fb5bb5d0adf945a5d5f5d187506a1/pint/default_en.txt#L89-L91) probably need to be changed, to add `ureg.mole` in their denominators.

If this is desired, I can put together a PR for it. I could see it breaking a bunch of code, but I believe it would make it more physically consistent.

print(pint.__file__)

  File "test.py", line 5, in <module>
    ur = pint.UnitRegistry()
  File "/Users/XXX/Desktop/fööbär/pint/registry.py", line 75, in __call__
    obj._after_init()
  File "/Users/XXX/Desktop/fööbär/pint/registry.py", line 1269, in _after_init

issue_title: Quantities using multi-dimensional ndarrays fail to render using IPython.lib.latextools.latex_to_png
issue_body:
Let's start with the problem code. If you're using the Spyder IPython console (and I'd guess the IPython Qt console, though I haven't tested this), output of certain Quantities will fail:

```
In [1]: Q_(np.zeros((2, 3)), 'V')
Out[1]: Failed to render latex: 
$[[ 0.  0.  0.]
^
Expected end of text (at char 0), (line:1, col:1) 
```

The IPython console detects `Quantity._repr_latex_()` and uses its output, which contains newlines from `format(some_multidimensional_array)`. Spyder's IPython console uses `IPython.lib.latextools.latex_to_png()` to render the latex (unlike the IPython notebook, which instead uses `latex_to_html()`). This ultimately uses matplotlib's TeX parser, which chokes on multi-line input.

The parsing method that the IPython notebook uses does not choke on the newlines, but it does seem to strip them out.

The simplest course of action that makes sense to me is to remove any newlines in `Quantity._repr_latex_()` before returning the result.

issue_title: Bad formed .py file for ENTITY-MIB
issue_body:
(Not sure if this issue should be filed here or under the pysnmp project)

I am experiencing problems when trying to use pysnmp with the ENTITY-MIB. It seems pysmi  is building an incorrect .py file for the ENTITY-MIB. 

To reproduce, make sure that the .py for ENTITY-MIB has not been generated before. Place the ENTITY-MIB, and its requirements in a folder. I downloaded all of them from http://mibs.snmplabs.com/asn1/:

```
wget http://mibs.snmplabs.com/asn1/ENTITY-MIB
wget http://mibs.snmplabs.com/asn1/SNMP-FRAMEWORK-MIB
wget http://mibs.snmplabs.com/asn1/SNMPv2-CONF
wget http://mibs.snmplabs.com/asn1/SNMPv2-SMI
wget http://mibs.snmplabs.com/asn1/SNMPv2-TC
```
Now, try to compile and load the mib:

```
from pysnmp.smi import builder, view, compiler
import pysnmp

mibBuilder = pysnmp.smi.builder.MibBuilder()
compiler.addMibCompiler(mibBuilder, sources=("/home",))
com = mibBuilder.getMibCompiler()
com.compile("ENTITY-MIB")
mibBuilder.loadModule("ENTITY-MIB")
```

Which, to me, raises an exception:

`MibLoadError: MIB module "/root/.pysnmp/mibs/ENTITY-MIB.py" load error: ['Traceback (most recent call last):\n', '  File "/usr/local/lib/python2.7/dist-packages/pysnmp/smi/builder.py", line 331, in loadModule\n    exec (modData, g)\n', '  File "<string>", line 127\n', "    entConfigChange = NotificationType((1, 3, 6, 1, 2, 1, 47, 2, 0, 1))if mibBuilder.loadTexts: entConfigChange.setStatus('current')\n",`

I hope you can reproduce the problem.

issue_title: Matching rules are never removed from the bus
issue_body: DBusClientConnection's delMatch should remove rules from the bus by calling RemoveMatch with the original rule string. Otherwise, they can keep accumulating until the bus refuses to add any more rules.

issue_title: Cleanup functionality needed for win-platform for singletons (socket, global_clock)
issue_body:
**Hi!**

First of all,
  while writing something at dlib site, **thanks so much** for this high quality library !

  We are using dlib as part of the shyft open-source project, at https://github.com/statkraft/shyft.
  It has excellent quality, header-only, well documented, multiplatform, and very well maintained.

We encountered something that we think is win-platform specific issue that we tracked to 
the function

dlib::get_global_clock().

That function maintains the singleton for timer_global_clock(), and works as expected for all practical purposes. We use dlib socket/streams and server, and it most likely gets activated for those parts.

The only problem we have found is when  we use the dlib as part of a python extension library.
When we exit python,  the python interpreter
 hangs forever in a lock/unlock during delete of the timer_global_clock singleton object.

The debugger attached picks up the thread/callstack located to:
 line 168..169 in shared_ptr_thread_safe.h 
  shared_node->unlock(); 
  delete data;

The callstack indicates that it's during the extension-library unload process, maybe static object destructors are called.

More specific info:
 only on windows platform (ms c++ 2015&2017, python 3.4..3.5.3),
 We are not able to reproduce on any linux-platform nor python version.

We found a workaround, that could be formed as a pull-request:
We just exposed a new function:

dlib::delete_global_clock();

- that reset the global-thread-safe singleton shared-pointer.
  (it's available at the fork https://github.com/sigbjorn/dlib)

If  we invoke this function as part of the python atexit registered function in our extension-module,
everything cleans up nice, and no more hang-situations. 

I suspect the dlib author(s) could provide even better solutions to this, maybe  with no code-changes,
so I leave this as an issue/question to maybe get some hint's about better approaches for solving this.

Br.
Sigbjørn Helset
MSc, Chief Developer Statkraft/ maintainer of shyft

issue_title: JSGString incorrectly matches None
issue_body:
`JSGStringMeta` does a pattern match against a the string representation of the target instance.  If the target instance is None, the match is applied to the string "None".  None should either map to the empty string or not map at all

```python
from pyjsg.jsglib.jsg import JSGString, JSGPattern
class S(JSGString):
    pattern = JSGPattern(r'[a-zA-Z]+')
    
isinstance('abc', S)
   True
isinstance('abc1', S)
   False
isinstance('', S)
  False
isinstance(None, S)
   True   # <--- Wrong
```

issue_title: Backend class prioritization
issue_body:
I'd like to do some architectural re-organization.

Instead of the existing implementation, which requires a backend class to be instantiated before it can be determined to be suitable, let's implement the 'supported' aspect as a classmethod, so classes can be determined to be suitable or not before instantiation.

Furthermore, instead of the supported attribute returning numbers -1, 0, and 1, let's supply instead a 'priority', which is a numeric value that can vary based on the environment. It should return None if it's not suitable and a positive number otherwise.

----------------------------------------
- Bitbucket: https://bitbucket.org/kang/python-keyring-lib/issue/80
- Originally reported by: Jason R. Coombs
- Originally created at: 2013-01-04T15:31:31.980

issue_title: Users without $HOME can't have config files
issue_body:
With the current setup, there is no way to specify a custom config file. This is problematic in that users without a $HOME as essentially unable to use keyring.

As an example, I have a server that runs a few daemon processes that require access to passwords, usually confined to their own virtualenv. For security purposes, many of these daemons run under their own user, which (appropriately) can't access my main keyring config file, but I don't seem to have a good way to create a separate keyring config file that they *can* access.

I don't want to hardcode passwords into the scripts they run, especially as some of the files are in version control / pushed to shared repositories. I also don't want to make a separate $HOME for each of the daemons, just so keyring will work (as it stores its config file there).

I think this may have been introduced by the fix of [Issue 22](https://bitbucket.org/kang/python-keyring-lib/issue/22/using-a-config-file-from-the-current). Would love to have a way to specify a custom config file at runtime, which I could have owned by the daemon user and only hold the passwords necessary for that daemon.

----------------------------------------
- Bitbucket: https://bitbucket.org/kang/python-keyring-lib/issue/154
- Originally reported by: Nathan Henrie
- Originally created at: 2014-12-19T22:02:13.012

issue_title: Feature Request: Implicit Typecasting
issue_body:
I think a great feature would be the option to attempt a typecast if `@enforce.runtime_validation` fails, either as a separate decorator `@enforce.runtime_typecasting` or as an optional parameter `@enforce.runtime_validation(coerce=True)`

A simple example might be

```
@enforce.runtime_typecasting
def elongator(s: str):
    return s * 10
...
elongator(5)
# returns '5555555555'
```

For the simple primitives, this could be a simple task.  For more complicated objects, an attempt to pass the argument itself to the required type as a single arg, as `*args`, and as `**kwargs` could be attempted in sequence.

```
class SimpleClass:
    def __init__(self, x: int):
        self.value = x

@enforce.runtime_typecasting
def SillySimpleClassFactory(x: SimpleClass):
    return x
...
SillySimpleClassFactory(10)
# returns SimpleClass(10)
```

```
class ComplicatedClass:
    def __init__(self, first_arg, second_arg, third_arg):
        ...
...
@enforce.runtime_typecasting
def ComplicatedClassFactory(x: ComplicatedClass):
    return x

ComplicatedClassFactory((1, 2, 3))
# returns ComplicatedClass(1, 2, 3)

ComplicatedClassFactory({'first_arg' : 4, 'third_arg' : 6, 'second_arg' : 5})
# returns ComplicatedClass(4, 5, 6)
```

I imagine `Callable` wouldn't be properly supported.  Probably others.

I would be happy to contribute this addition myself in a month or two.

issue_title: autopep8 should not add a blank line before a class docstring (PEP 257)
issue_body:
As referenced in issues #83, #88, and #178, an earlier version of PEP 257 made the weird recommendation of inserting a blank line before the docstring of a class.

However, this recommendation has since been removed. The current PEP 257 only recommends adding a blank line after the docstring, not before:

> Insert a blank line after all docstrings (one-line or multi-line) that document a class -- generally speaking, the class's methods are separated from each other by a single blank line, and the docstring needs to be offset from the first method by a blank line.

(The commit that changed it: [9b715d8246db](https://hg.python.org/peps/rev/9b715d8246db))

As far as i understand, autopep8's E309 fixer does not currently distinguish between docstrings  and other class members when inserting blank lines, so this issue cannot be worked around with `--ignore=E309` alone, if you still want autopep8 to insert blank lines before non-docstring members.

issue_title: Flaky test: `test_original_decoded_response_is_not_modified`
issue_body:
There have been a few failures of this test since its introduction (see #247 for some comments around this).

It's difficult to reproduce locally, but this does happen in Travis. It may have to do with differences in responses from httpbin.org/gzip, but without more verbose logging in Travis it's hard to say for sure. 

We can introduce an assertion error message that logs the decompressed content, but that'll be truncated by the current test runner (unless we run it with `-v`). If we're doing that, we might as well just decompress the content and assert on the decompressed content.

I can send out a PR for comparing the decompressed data if that seems reasonable.

issue_title: Improve netmiko syslog configuration
Timing out on authentication if primary auth is unavailable
issue_body:
In certain circumstances a user may use TACACS, RADIUS, etc as their primary authentication service (with local being their backup).  In normal operation this should work fine.  In the event TACACS or RADIUS is unavailable there will be a delay in authentication.  Even though we can set a timeout for SSH being established, we cannot set a timeout for authentication success.  I have tested this a few times and I always receive an exception of NetMikoTimeoutException("Timed out waiting for recv_ready").  

base_connection.py, on establish_connection, this gets called; if self.wait_for_recv_ready_newline():
The problem is there is no way for a user to customize the wait time for initial authentication.  I would ask either use the timeout value set, or allow us to use global_delay_factor.

Using Cisco device configured for two RADIUS servers with a timeout of 2 and retransmit of 2.  I use RADIUS as the primary and local as the backup.

issue_title: Auto discovery enhancement
issue_body:
What do you think about enchaning the auto discovery module created in the beginning of 2017 by using SSH discovery instead of SNMP.

The issue I see in using SNMP is that we need to use an extra protocol and extra credentials to perform auto-discovery. This means that auto-discovery only works if you have SNMP enabled on the asset.

I was thinking about doing auto-discovery over a preliminary SSH connection, this way:

1. Try to connect over SSH port using paramiko
2. Use SSH authentication with provided credentials (or maybe application authentication as needed by Cisco WLC)
3. Execute several "discovery commands" (i.e '*show version | inc IOS*' on Cisco IOS or '*show version | inc Nexus*' or Cisco NX-OS, etc). The idea is that the command's output does not span longer than the default terminal length.
4. Close the SSH connection
5. Based on the output string, return the corresponding driver to be used with the **ConnectHandler** class.

This way we are only using one unique set of credential for both discovery and real SSH connection for interacting with the asset.

Do you think it's worth working on it ?

issue_title: Build action doesn't properly compare date version parts to integer version parts
TorProcessProtocol needs a quit() or exit()
issue_body:
From conversation with @aagbsn there isn't a good way to wait for a Tor process that txtorcon created to "really exit". This can become a problem in integration tests.

For release-1.x style API: the ``Tor`` class should have a ``quit`` or similar method that:
  - sends QUIT to the Tor
  - waits for protocol disconnect
  - (if we launched the tor) ``signalProcess('TERM')`` and wait for ``processExited`` to be called (i.e. the method below from ``TorProcessProtocol``)
  - fires Deferred we returned

For 0.x (current) APIs, ``TorProcessProtocol`` should grow a ``quit`` method that does:
 - ``signalProcess('TERM')`` 
 -  wait for ``processExited`` to be called
 - fires Deferred we returned

The latter would be re-used by the release-1.x-style API.

issue_title: [artstation] site support discussion
issue_body:
An issue concerning the various details of https://www.artstation.com/.

Initial work has been done in https://github.com/mikf/gallery-dl/commit/723cc66bb1dd3c765f41fcc0d1fdf3d90e77e4e6, which adds support for

- A user's artworks or projects (Artstation uses these two terms interchangeably)
  - https://www.artstation.com/ivangraphics/
  - https://ivangraphics.artstation.com/
  - https://ivangraphics.artstation.com/projects/
- Individual artworks
  - https://www.artstation.com/artwork/Db3dy
  - https://ivangraphics.artstation.com/projects/Db3dy
- A user's liked artworks
  - https://www.artstation.com/ivangraphics/likes

This list is probably incomplete, but I'm not entirely sure what else there is. Please add any missing image-categories or URL schemes for the listed categories if you can.

Known issues:
- The original images uploaded by artists are no longer accessible. You are only allowed to view and download a lower quality version.
  In the past you could replace the `/large/` of any image URL with `/original/` to request the original image, but that was disabled - apparently due to bandwidth issues.
  - Large: https://cdna.artstation.com/p/assets/images/images/009/672/856/large/ivan-tantsiura-books.jpg?1520274197
  - Original (403 Forbidden): https://cdna.artstation.com/p/assets/images/images/009/672/856/original/ivan-tantsiura-books.jpg?1520274197
- Artstation uses Cloudflare to distribute their images, which in turn uses ["Polish"](https://blog.cloudflare.com/introducing-polish-automatic-image-optimizati/) to reduce filesize without noticeably reducing image quality. This means they either strip metadata or even re-compress an image.
  Folks at Danbooru found out that you can still get the "unpolished" version by sending a randomized query parameter to cause a cache miss which has [already been implemented](https://github.com/mikf/gallery-dl/blob/723cc66bb1dd3c765f41fcc0d1fdf3d90e77e4e6/gallery_dl/extractor/artstation.py#L92).

Interesting threads discussing Artstation on danbooru:
[1] https://danbooru.donmai.us/forum_topics/13057
[2] https://danbooru.donmai.us/forum_topics/14952

issue_title: Would tinydb be better if we use JSON Lines format?
issue_body:
I just gave tinydb a try. One thing I noticed the format is like this:

`{"_default":{"1":{"a":1},"2":{"b":2}}}`

So it is likely that tinydb has to parse the entire object in memory. Have you considered JSON Lines format (http://jsonlines.org/examples/)?

For an example:
```
{"_default":{"1":{"a":1}}}
{"_default":{"2":{"b":2}}}
...
```

I haven't thought of all the cases, but the advantage is when doing search in a large database file, we can iterate over the file line by line instead of reading and parsing the entire file.

This is just an idea. Have a nice day!
Hieu

I saw that whitespace and case-agnostic prefixes were taken care of recently. 👍 I'll make note of any remaining 1.13 issues here seeing as you're active and able to implement them into the official repo.

Remaining issues for 1.13 support:

1. Something important to note is that whitespace was not valid (in SNBT) until 1.13, so you may wish to either (1) add a parameter to the parser or (2) create a separate branch for legacy (1.12 and prior) support (or vice-versa).
2. Long arrays are now a thing (apparently part of the new region file format), so probably `[L` should be added to the list of tokens and parsed accordingly.

I think that takes care of everything but there may still be changes before 1.13 is fully released.

issue_title: Definition for namedtuple is wrong
issue_body:
I've noticed that jedi-vim opens the current dir when trying to goto a namedtuple class.

The following tries to reproduce this, showing two issues:

```python
source = """
from collections import namedtuple

Foo = namedtuple('Foo', 'id timestamp gps_timestamp attributes')
Foo
"""

from jedi.api import Script

lines = source.split("\n")
d = Script(source, line=len(lines)-1, column=1).goto_definitions()
d1 = d[0]

print(d1)
print(d1.line)
print(d1.module_path)
print(d1.get_line_code())
assert d1.line == 4
```

Output:
```
<Definition class Foo>
5
…/vim/plugged/jedi-vim/pythonx/jedi
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "t-namedtuple.py", line 16, in <module>
    print(d1.get_line_code())
  File "…/vim/plugged/jedi-vim/pythonx/jedi/jedi/api/classes.py", line 381, in get_line_code
    lines = parser_cache[self._evaluator.grammar._hashed][path].lines
KeyError: '…/vim/plugged/jedi-vim/pythonx/jedi'
```

1. the line is expected to be 4, not 5?!
2. `get_line_code` should not crash?!
3. `module_path` is `…/vim/plugged/jedi-vim/pythonx/jedi`, which is the directory of the file, and the reason for jedi-vim to open it

issue_title: Infinite loop in DiffParser
issue_body:
Suppose test.py contains the following:

```python
x = str(
    True)

a = 1

def foo():
    pass

b = 2
```

Vimrc:

```
execute pathogen#infect()
syntax on
filetype plugin indent on
let g:jedi#show_call_signatures = 2
set noshowmode
```

Steps to reproduce:
* Open test.py in Vim and press A to start inserting at end of line 1. Note that call signature of str is shown.
* Press `<Esc>`Js to move "True" up a line and start inserting right before True on line 1. Note that call signature of str is again shown.
* Press uuA to move "True" back down and start inserting at the end of line 1.

Expected result:
* Call signature of str shown.

Actual result:
* Vim hangs, CPU pegged at 100%.

Happens on both jedi master (fc27ca1b6ada9ac9f70fd38a4f7f803b40d1572e 'fix a couple of error locations') and dev (a859add6d7d86f3628cfe14f51dd1ae5addee65f Only resolve names for actual modules).

Git bisect suggests that b0f340748cad3555bfa9c2481f70fff02c370e5c "So much work for one simple diff fail." introduced the bug. The commit just before (b779677bf9f32a90f9a31203c91c08ca034f6859) does not exhibit the problem.

If I insert `assert from_ <= to, (self._copied_ranges, (from_, to))` right after [`self._copied_ranges.append((from_, to))`](https://github.com/davidhalter/jedi/blob/b0f340748cad3555bfa9c2481f70fff02c370e5c/jedi/parser/diff.py#L257-L262), then I get the following assertion failure traceback:

issue_title: Releasing the parser as a separate project
issue_body:
Based on some discussions in #630 and having my own ideas as well, I've been planning to release Jedi's parser to the public for a while now (as a separate pypi package). There is now a prototype of how that could look like. If you're interested, please test it and tell me what you think.

I want to know how the API could be different (better!), what you're missing and what you would remove.

The current prototype is in the dev branch under `jedi/parser/`. You probably only need the Python part, because the parser generator behind the whole thing will be able to understand different types of grammars.

Most people will want to use the `from jedi.parser.python import parse` function. It's pretty powerful.

Unfortunately there's no good documentation, yet. But if you're willing to PR a few changes, I'm thankful for all the help.

issue_title: Support SSL
issue_body:
When I try to connect to Windows Azure SQL, it raises the following exception:

```
AttributeError: '_TdsSession' object has no attribute '_sock'
```

Changing line 3199 on file tds.py from:

```
self._sock = ssl.wrap_socket(self._sock, ssl_version=ssl.PROTOCOL_SSLv3)
```

to:

```
self._sock = ssl.wrap_socket(self._transport._sock, ssl_version=ssl.PROTOCOL_SSLv3)
```

Solves the issue. But then I get the following error:

```
ssl.SSLError: [Errno 8] _ssl.c:507: EOF occurred in violation of protocol
```

Has anyone had success when connecting to Azure using pytds?

issue_title: psutil compatibility with linux subsystem for windows
issue_body:
Running glances on ubuntu in linux subsystem for windows I get several errors.
Apparently there is no `/proc/vmstat`, no `/etc/mtab`.

issue_title: [XEP-0390] Implement a re-usable, etree-based hash calculator
issue_body:
This is with the thought that other XMPP clients using an etree-compatible XML library can re-use the code, thus easing adoption.

We should be using the etree-based captured XML data (InfoQuery is a CapturingXSO) anyways to ensure that ecaps work properly independent of other XSO definitions.

issue_title: Auto suffix adding on email authentication - why?
issue_body:
With this code:
`import yagmail


def send_email():
    """Send an email"""
    yag = yagmail.SMTP(user='user@mycompany.com', password='password',
                       host='mail.mycompany.com', port=587, 
                       smtp_starttls = False, smtp_skip_login = True) # also tried port 25

    attachments = []
    yag.send('user@mycompany.com', 'Some text', ['The content of the email'], attachments)


if __name__ == '__main__':
    send_email()


# I see the error:
# smtplib.SMTPSenderRefused: (501, b'5.1.7 Invalid address', '@gmail.com')`

Why is it adding @gmail.com when I choose to smtp_skip_login?  I simply want to avoid all authentication, gmail is not relevant here.

issue_title: strip output extension data/metadata
issue_body:
Would it be possible to have an option to strip out extension data as well?

Different users use different extensions and in different ways and currently nbstripout doesn't strip that data, causing conflicts or/and unnecessary commit noise.

Examples: 

a) if I use ToC extension, I end up with:

    -   "toc_position": {},
    +   "toc_position": {
    +    "height": "calc(100% - 180px)",
    +    "left": "10px",
    +    "top": "150px",
    +    "width": "290.391px"
    +   },
        "toc_section_display": "block",
    -   "toc_window_display": false
    +   "toc_window_display": true

this is a  "toc": entry. Perhaps there is a known set of jupyter core top-level entries that can be kept and then all the extension top-level entries removed during stripout?

b) If I use Collapse Headers extension - it adds a bunch of metadata noise:

       {
        "cell_type": "markdown",
    -   "metadata": {},
    +   "metadata": {
    +    "heading_collapsed": true
    +   },
        "source": [
         "# Fin"
        ]
    @@ -1154,7 +1310,9 @@
       {
        "cell_type": "code",
        "execution_count": null,
    -   "metadata": {},
    +   "metadata": {
    +    "hidden": true
    +   },
        "outputs": [],
        "source": []

Perhaps there can be an option to force to set metadata to {}? Which would solve this particular extension.

Thank you!

issue_title: Problems linking to libz
issue_body:
Hey!

I just compiled the damn thing and got:
ImportError: /home/david/Descargas/nassl/test/nassl/_nassl.so: undefined symbol: zError

It seems to be statically linking to libz.a, which is what we expect, since ldd nassl.so shows no trace of libz. I don't have libz.a in my system, so it must be linking against the internal one.
An "nm" on libz.a shows that zError is there, so I have no idea what could possibly be wrong.

Thoughts?
Thanks!

issue_title: Terminal detection?
issue_body:
Does colorama detect the terminals capabilities and suppresses colors accordingly?

I am using `structlog` from logging, which uses colorama in turn. When executing my script using `popen`, log colors are not displayed. I am not trying to understand whether it's colorama that's suppressing colors (and if yes, whether I can force it) or if popen eats the colors (nom nom).

issue_title: Colorama does not work in MinGW64
issue_body:
Hello,

it seams that MinGW64 is not recognized as a ANSI color code compatible system.
Normal coloring in bash (e.g. the prompt or `ls`) is working.

P.S. The list of supported shells on Windows doesn't contain PowerShell. As far as I can tell it's working fine. At least the colors work, haven't used other codes like cursor codes, yet.)

Regards
    Patrick

issue_title: KafkaConsumer always passes message key to deserializer, even if key is None
issue_body:
I specified `json.loads` as the `KafkaConsumer`'s `key_deserializer` callable, then produced a message without a key.

`json.loads` throws an exception about expecting a string. Looks like root cause is here: https://github.com/dpkp/kafka-python/blob/master/kafka/consumer/fetcher.py#L546

Fetcher is always using passing the key to the deserializer, even if the key is `None`.

Will submit a PR shortly.

issue_title: Crashs after long time running the glances --browser (recursion exceeded)
issue_body:
Hello,

Sorry for this unusual bug report.

Currently i'm using the Glances v2.8.8

After some times (hours) keeping running the "glances --browser" it's always crashing with the message: "maximum recursion depth exceeded while calling a Python object" (the complete dump is in the end of this message)

Investigating the problem I found that the method __serve_forever in GlancesClientBrowser class in client_browser.py runs recursively for ever, so after running during some time this recursion finally exceeds the python recursion limit giving the crashes:
```
def __serve_forever(self):
        """Main client loop."""
        # No need to update the server list
        # It's done by the GlancesAutoDiscoverListener class (autodiscover.py)
        # Or define staticaly in the configuration file (module static_list.py)
        # For each server in the list, grab elementary stats (CPU, LOAD, MEM, OS...)

        logger.debug("Iter through the following server list: {}".format(self.get_servers_list()))
        for v in self.get_servers_list():
            thread = threading.Thread(target=self.__update_stats, args=[v])
            thread.start()

        # Update the screen (list or Glances client)
        if self.screen.active_server is None:
            #  Display the Glances browser
            self.screen.update(self.get_servers_list())
        else:
            # Display the active server
            self.__display_server(self.get_servers_list()[self.screen.active_server])

        # Loop
        self.__serve_forever()
```

To fix it I made change my glances client_browser.py removing the recursive loop and using a while instead:
```
 def __serve_forever(self):
        """Main client loop."""
        # No need to update the server list
        # It's done by the GlancesAutoDiscoverListener class (autodiscover.py)
        # Or define staticaly in the configuration file (module static_list.py)
        # For each server in the list, grab elementary stats (CPU, LOAD, MEM, OS...)

        while True:

                logger.debug("Iter through the following server list: {}".format(self.get_servers_list()))
                for v in self.get_servers_list():
                    thread = threading.Thread(target=self.__update_stats, args=[v])
                    thread.start()

                # Update the screen (list or Glances client)
                if self.screen.active_server is None:
                    #  Display the Glances browser
                    self.screen.update(self.get_servers_list())
                else:
                    # Display the active server
                    self.__display_server(self.get_servers_list()[self.screen.active_server])

        # Loop
        #self.__serve_forever()
```

This fixed the problem for me, so now I'm here posting this bug report to help any other that tried to use glances has a multi-server monitor.

issue_title: openssl issue with glances-2.7

I am using glances 2.7.1.1-2  on our debian-stretch system.

issue_title: CRITICAL: InfluxDB database did not exist
issue_body:
Building from dev branch : 
* Ubuntu 14.04.2
* commit 929b832b74ba874d4add74c6478fafab859db998
* Python 2.7.6 (in a venv)
* pip 6.0.8
* InfluxDB 0.8.6

The same error occurs when building from v2.3 tag

```bash
> .venv/bin/pip install -r requirements.txt
> .venv/bin/pip install influxdb #needed since influxdb lib is not in the requierements
> .venv/bin/python setup.py install
[...]
Installed .venv/lib/python2.7/site-packages/Glances-2.4b0-py2.7.egg
Processing dependencies for Glances==2.4b0
Searching for psutil==2.2.1
Best match: psutil 2.2.1
Adding psutil 2.2.1 to easy-install.pth file

Using .venv/lib/python2.7/site-packages
Finished processing dependencies for Glances==2.4b0
```

```bash
> .venv/bin/glances --export-influxdb -C influx.conf
.venv/local/lib/python2.7/site-packages/influxdb/client.py:451: FutureWarning: get_database_list is deprecated, and will be removed in future versions. Please use ``InfluxDBClient.get_list_database`` instead.
  FutureWarning)
CRITICAL: InfluxDB database 'glances' did not exist. Please create it
```

BUT : my db exists :'(
```bash
> influxdb-cli -h dev.influx.acme.org -u root -p root -d glances --pretty
glances> Connecting to http://dev.influx.acme.org:8086/db/glances ...
glances> ✔ ready
glances> list series
┌─────────────────┬─────────────────┬──────┐
│       time      │ sequence_number │ name │
└─────────────────┴─────────────────┴──────┘
Query took  2 ms
```

my influx.conf file :
```
[influxdb]
host=dev.influx.acme.org
port=8086
user=root
password=root
db=glances
```

issue_title: Including battery and AC adapter health in Glances
issue_body:
#### Description

Please add critical extended battery (ACPI / laptop) information to Glances in order to monitor battery health. This information should include—where available—voltage _versus_ design voltage and battery capacity _versus_ design capacity. This should be available as both raw data and as calculated percentages. ACPI battery information provided can vary based on manufacturer, kernel revision, and so forth. Where possible, the battery manufacture date and/or calculated age should be displayed if the data are available to Glances. Similarly, temperature (if available) and voltage / voltage _versus_ design voltage should be provided.

It may be desirable to evaluate the temperature based on well-known lithium-ion safe operating temperature ranges. Providing informational messages about charging at suboptimal temperatures would be welcome. Based on multiple sources, the lithium-cobalt chemistry used in most common laptop batteries should not be charged at temperatures near the end of the range 0-45°C to avoid reducing the battery lifespan. Similarly, discharge temperatures near or above 60°C appear to be best avoided. Lithium-cobalt chemistry becomes dangerous at about 130°C, and charge management ICs in batteries should normally disable the battery well before this point; however, advance warnings to the user would be beneficial, perhaps around 90°C.

Finally, please provide the AC adapter state.

#### Versions

n/a

#### Logs

All of the necessary information appears to be available from sysfs on all of the portable Linux systems I've tested. Here's some example output, and then nearly the same information as formatted by the upower(1) tool:
```
$ cat /sys/class/power_supply/BAT0/uevent
POWER_SUPPLY_NAME=BAT0
POWER_SUPPLY_STATUS=Unknown
POWER_SUPPLY_PRESENT=1
POWER_SUPPLY_TECHNOLOGY=Li-ion
POWER_SUPPLY_CYCLE_COUNT=0

POWER_SUPPLY_CHARGE_NOW=7301000
POWER_SUPPLY_CAPACITY=96
POWER_SUPPLY_CAPACITY_LEVEL=Normal
POWER_SUPPLY_MODEL_NAME=DELL PT43485
POWER_SUPPLY_MANUFACTURER=Samsung SDI
POWER_SUPPLY_SERIAL_NUMBER=[REMOVED]
```
```
$ upower -i /org/freedesktop/UPower/devices/battery_BAT0
  native-path:          BAT0
  vendor:               Samsung SDI
  model:                DELL PT43485
  serial:               [REMOVED]

  updated:              Fri 10 Mar 2017 04:29:14 PM EST (71 seconds ago)

    present:             yes

AC adapter information is available from several sources, again including sysfs:
```
$ cat /sys/class/power_supply/AC/online

```

$ acpitool -a

issue_title: Example code seems to be wrong
issue_body:
https://python-mss.readthedocs.io/en/dev/examples.html#playing-with-pixels

In this example, `sct.monitor` is passed directly to `grab` method.
In osx, `grab` changes a value of `monitor` param. As a result of it, the value of `sct.monitor` is also changed because python passes reference of `dict` rather than copying it.
(https://github.com/BoboTiG/python-mss/blob/master/mss/darwin.py#L182)

I think it's better to copy the dict before passing it to `grab`.
```
sct_img = sct.grab(sct.monitors[1].copy())
```

issue_title: Suspending tasks with no task manager
issue_body:
In the **task** module, it is acceptable to install a task with no task manager created, the code just adds the task to the list of uninstalled tasks and they will be installed when the task manager is created.  However, when a task is installed and then suspended, the call to suspend_task() fails.

Fix the code to remove the task from the uninstalled tasks if there is no task manager to delegate the activity.

issue_title: ARM support
issue_body:
Upstream CEF added ARM 32-bit support in September. [Upstream Issue #1990](https://bitbucket.org/chromiumembedded/cef/issues/1990/linux-add-arm-build-support) provides more details. Below is a short summary:

* Supported branches are 2840 and later (v54 and later). The corresponding cefpython branches are cefpython54, cefpython55 and later.
* Currently there is X11 dependency. ARM build tested to run successfully on an ASUS C201 Chromebook running Crouton/Ubuntu Xfce.
* X11 dependency is to be removed in the future and this will allow to run on e.g. Raspberry Pi
* You can cross-compile on Ubuntu, instructions are provided in the CEF issue linked above
* ARM64 cross-compile is to be added when Chromium Issue 613452 is resolved

To build CEF for ARM (cross-compile on Ubuntu) there will be modifications required to cefpython's tools/automate.py script:
* Add --arm-build flag which will call upstream automate-git.py with that flag
* Add "arm_float_abi=hard" in GN_DEFINES when --arm-build flag is passed

Currently CEF Python has issue231 and issue251 patches that must be applied to CEF for the CEF binaries to work with cefpython. So you should use the tools/automate.py script to build CEF which applies patches automatically. These two patches will be removed when they get merged into upstream, see the NOTES section at the end of Issue #264.

I haven't yet played with ARM. Looks like getting a Chromebook and then using Crouton to install Ubuntu Xfce is the way to go.

issue_title: AttributeException When Iterating Through Multiple Strategies
issue_body:
I am using Backtrader to test large numbers of previously generated strategies that are stored in a postgresql database. The strategy is pulled from the database and tested by backtrader, then the TradeAnalyzer and Sharpe analyzers are applied, with their outputs being once again pushed to the database. I began to run into issues where the program would thrown an AtrributeError exception after running an arbitrary number of tests:

`Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "C:/Users/Nelson/PycharmProjects/blackbox_revival/executor.py", line 10, in <module>
    testspace.execute()
  File "C:\Users\Nelson\PycharmProjects\blackbox_revival\testspace.py", line 82, in execute
    runtime=cerebro.run()
  File "C:\Users\Nelson\Miniconda3\envs\py342\lib\site-packages\backtrader\cerebro.py", line 788, in run
    runstrat = self.runstrategies(iterstrat)
  File "C:\Users\Nelson\Miniconda3\envs\py342\lib\site-packages\backtrader\cerebro.py", line 845, in runstrategies
    data.preload()
  File "C:\Users\Nelson\Miniconda3\envs\py342\lib\site-packages\backtrader\feed.py", line 626, in preload
    self.f.close()
AttributeError: 'NoneType' object has no attribute 'close'`

Attempting to diagnose the problem, I wrote a test file which follows the examples in the documentation as closely as possible. The 'executor'.py module contains a simple for loop which calls the execute function in the test file

```
from __future__ import (absolute_import, division, print_function,
                        unicode_literals)
import datetime
import backtrader as bt
import backtrader.analyzers as btanalyzers

class RunBack(bt.Strategy):
    def __init__(self):
        self.dataclose = self.datas[0].close
        self.dataopen = self.datas[0].open
        self.datahigh = self.datas[0].high
        self.datalow = self.datas[0].low
        self.datavolume = self.datas[0].volume
        self.dataopeninterest = self.datas[0].openinterest
        self.openprice = 0
        self.maxprice = 0
        print('Testing Strategy')

    def next(self):
        #LONG CONDITIONS
        if not self.position:
            try:
                if self.dataclose[-3] == self.datahigh[-3]: self.buycontract()
            except:
                cerebro.runstop()
        #CLOSE CONDITIONS
        if self.position:
            if self.datahigh[0] > self.maxprice:
                self.maxprice = self.datahigh[0]

            if self.datalow[0] < (self.maxprice-2.5) or self.datalow[0] < self.openprice-2.5:
                self.maxprice = self.openprice=0
                RunBack.flatten(self)

    def buycontract(self):
        #print("BOUGHT 1 TO OPEN @ %.2f" % self.dataclose[0])
        self.openprice = self.maxprice = self.dataclose[0]
        self.buy()
    def flatten(self):
        #print("WENT FLAT @ %.2f" % self.dataclose[0])
        self.sell()

cerebro = bt.Cerebro()
cerebro.addstrategy(RunBack)

data = bt.feeds.YahooFinanceData(
    dataname = 'GLD',
    fromdate = datetime.datetime(2015, 1, 1),
    todate = datetime.datetime(2016, 10, 14))
cerebro.adddata(data)
cerebro.addanalyzer(btanalyzers.SharpeRatio, _name = 'sharperatio')
cerebro.addanalyzer(btanalyzers.TradeAnalyzer, _name = 'profittracking')

def execute():
    cerebro.broker.setcash(100000.0)
    runtime = cerebro.run()
    runtimeresult = runtime[0]
    #Number of Trades Made
    tradesmade= runtimeresult.analyzers.profittracking.get_analysis()['total']['total']
    print('SUCCESSFUL BACKTEST')

```
The issue does not appear after a consistent number of backtest have been performed, and appears irrespective of current cpu/memory load. My best guess is that the loop isn't giving Cerebro enough time to shut down before calling it for the next test, but the issue persists even when a delay is added between executions. I'm kind of at a loss here. Do you have any suggestions as to how to more reliably set up the iteration within Backtrader?

Thank you!

Nelson

issue_title: Spur SshShell expects to be connected to a bash(like) session
issue_body:
Right now when you connect to a machine with a custom shell (which maybe is there to reject connections), spur's spawn function does not take into account that the shell may not be bash.  In my testing this results in raising an exception in `_read_int_line` as spur tries to parse the pid or `which` command output (all this in `spur/ssh.py`).

This came up for us when we tried using spur to connect to an ec2 machine with the root user.  The AMI in question had a custom shell which essentially looks like this (my version not theirs, but it accomplishes the same thing):

```
#!/bin/bash
echo 'Please login as the user "ubuntu" rather than the user "root".'
exit 1
```

Since the connection successfully opens, spur goes ahead and runs the command and then fails to parse the rejection line as an int and raises a ValueError:

```
ValueError: invalid literal for int() with base 10: b'Please login as the user "ubuntu" rather than the user "root".'
```

I am not sure what the correct fix is here, but it seems that somehow validating that the receiving process is `/bin/bash` (or a bash variant) would be good.

For now we are catching the `ValueError` in a spawn wrapper, but this is not ideal since it may mask other real `ValueErrors` in the future.

Thanks!

issue_title: 2.2.1 not on pypi
SharedMemory should permit file descriptor to be 0
issue_body:
Reported by Thomas T -- 
A valid file descriptor can be zero. If you close file descriptor 0 (usually stdin), then you will get 0 on the next open call. As far as I can see, your code assumes that the descriptor is positive or negative, and zero is an invalid file descriptor (lines 938 and 994 in posix_ipc_module.c). If you get descriptor 0 then `SharedMemory_close_fd' cannot close it (line 1127).

issue_title: leafref which is must be integer but error as string
issue_body:
In the below snippet the leaf subinterface is leafref pointing to the index which(also leafref) points to the config/index to "uint32" data type.

But the pybind class generated for this - calls this as "leafref" (string) which adds quotes and becomes string.

This breaks the YANG compliance where this data MUST be "uint32".

Note: jtox plugin works fine.
Same issue is with xsl as well.

leaf subinterface
 type leafref
  path /oc-if:interfaces/oc-if:interface[oc-if:name=current()/../interface]/oc-if:subinterfaces/oc-if:subinterface/oc-if:index
 description Reference to a subinterface -- this requires the base
interface to be specified using the interface leaf in
this container.  If only a reference to a base interface
is requuired, this leaf should not be set.

issue_title: 是否可以加入法定节假日和调休日区分呢？
issue_body:
按照国家加班工资规定：
     在法定节假日，用人单位应当按照不低于日工资基数或者小时工资基数的300％来支付加班工资，而且不得以调休、补休替代。在休息日，用人单位应当先安排同等时间的补休；确实不能安排补休的，要按照不低于日工资基数或者小时工资基数的200％支付加班工资。

    根据国家统一安排，2018年春节放假共七天，即2月15日至21日。其中，国家法定节假日有三天，即2月16日到18日，也就是农历正月初一、初二、初三；另外有四天是调休日，即2月15日、19日、20日、21日，这是由2月11日、17日、18日、24日这四个周末休息日调过来的。

也就是说，同样是节假日，法定假日和调休日的加班工资是不一样的。正常我们这边做个项目，就要根据这个区分考勤信息。

麻烦大神看下是否能支持？我不是专业搞python的。怕提交的代码不规范

issue_title: electrumX terminated with error: b'Corruption: bad entry in block'
issue_body:
A few weeks ago my SSD died while copying the bitcoin blockchain. I cloned the SSD to a new disk, somehow this was successful, and got my system running again. `bitcoind` re synced and all seam normal, but `electrumx v1.4.3` keeps crashing with error `b'Corruption: bad entry in block'`. Perhaps `bitcoind` blockchain files where corrupt, so I copied `blocks` and `chainstate` directories from another system to be sure, but electrumx keeps crashing. Then upgraded to latest `v1.8` using `electrumx-installer` script, but that also didn't solve the crash. Below shows log output from `journalctl -u electrumx`) of `v1.4.3` and subsequent `v1.8`.

Should I rebuild the db and is there a command for that?

my system: `Linux HPLaptop 4.9.0-6-amd64 #1 SMP Debian 4.9.88-1+deb9u1 (2018-05-07) x86_64 GNU/Linux`

```

The result is the same with the copy constructor (adding (const MyClass&))

Any ideas ?

Thank you

issue_title: Add support for refids to be global
issue_body:
The current implementation effectively puts the refids for types and such (and even labels) in a project-specific namespace by prefixing the generated id's with the project name.  While this works, it starts to become cumbersome to use when working with a project that is broken up into multiple code-bases.  Especially the auto-generated type links from class definitions have an issue here jumping across boundaries.  I was able to work around this by removing the project name from the generated refId, but this probably is not a good permanent solution, it may break other projects.

I'm guessing the better solution would be to have an option in the sphinx configuration that would allow the user to have project-specific refId's or global refId's.  I can work on the change I think, does it sound like a good idea?  Do we actually need the ability to change this or should all refId's be global?  I don't run into conflicts in my project since the code bases already have a dependency from one to the other so avoid name conflicts through namespaces, but that may not be the case in all projects.

Thoughts?

issue_title: PGN parser issue
issue_body:
Hi,

There seems to be a problem with [line 401 of the PGN parser code ](https://github.com/niklasf/python-chess/blob/24dfecdaca3df77312cb05e476e75ca9ba82075d/chess/pgn.py#L401). In the previous version of the code, there used to be a way for the board to be initialized in the STANDARD variant. See for example https://github.com/niklasf/python-chess/commit/c2c0390a629d7805e6d8cfc709d9daa6f3189b2b#diff-64a9eeb9d1bcfe6aa1eb017cc0f9f57eL397
In the latest commit, the library returns an error when I am parsing a standard game:

```
AttributeError                            Traceback (most recent call last)
<ipython-input-39-d8c83423e1c9> in <module>()
      4     from io import StringIO  # Python 3
      5 pgn = StringIO(pgn_string)
----> 6 carlsen_game = chess.pgn.read_game(pgn)

/Users/cmav/miniconda2/envs/chess/lib/python2.7/site-packages/chess/pgn.pyc in read_game(handle, Visitor)
    830     # Movetext parser state.
    831     try:
--> 832         board_stack = collections.deque([dummy_game.board()])
    833     except ValueError as error:
    834         visitor.handle_error(error)

/Users/cmav/miniconda2/envs/chess/lib/python2.7/site-packages/chess/pgn.pyc in board(self, _cache)
    399 
    400         fen = self.headers.get("FEN") if self.headers.get("SetUp", "1") == "1" else None
--> 401         board = VariantBoard(fen or VariantBoard.starting_fen, chess960)
    402         board.chess960 = board.chess960 or board.has_chess960_castling_rights()
    403         return board

AttributeError: type object 'Board' has no attribute 'starting_fen'

I think that starting_fen is only initialized for the non-standard games, and it has been omitted as attribute in the standard Board class.

issue_title: Can't draw arrows after a move is made
issue_body:
`chess.Board()._repr_svg_()` offers square highlighting but I want to mark the last moves with an arrow. How can I do that?

```
#! /usr/bin/env python

"""
This module is the execution point of the chess GUI application
named Chess Titan.
"""

import sys

import chess
import chess.svg

from PyQt5.QtCore import pyqtSlot, Qt
from PyQt5.QtSvg import QSvgWidget
from PyQt5.QtWidgets import QApplication, QWidget


class MainWindow(QWidget):
    """
    Create a surface for the chessboard.
    """
    def __init__(self):
        """
        Initialize the chessboard.
        """
        super().__init__()

        self.setWindowTitle("Chess GUI")
        self.setGeometry(300, 300, 800, 800)

        self.widgetSvg = QSvgWidget(parent=self)
        self.widgetSvg.setGeometry(10, 10, 600, 600)

        self.boardSize = min(self.widgetSvg.width(),
                             self.widgetSvg.height())
        self.coordinates = True
        self.margin = 0.05 * self.boardSize if self.coordinates else 0
        self.squareSize = (self.boardSize - 2 * self.margin) / 8.0
        self.pieceToMove = [None, None]

        self.board = chess.Board()
        self.startingPosition = None
        self.endingPosition = None

        self.drawBoard()



    @pyqtSlot(QWidget)
    def mousePressEvent(self, event):
        """
        Handle left mouse clicks and enable moving chess pieces by
        clicking on a chess piece and then the target square.

        Moves must be made according to the rules of chess because
        illegal moves are suppressed.
        """
        if event.x() <= self.boardSize and event.y() <= self.boardSize:
            if event.buttons() == Qt.LeftButton:
                if self.margin < event.x() < self.boardSize - self.margin and self.margin < event.y() < self.boardSize - self.margin:
                    self.file = int((event.x() - self.margin) / self.squareSize)
                    self.rank = 7 - int((event.y() - self.margin) / self.squareSize)
                    self.square = chess.square(self.file, self.rank)
                    self.piece = self.board.piece_at(self.square)
                    self.fileCharacter = chr(self.file + 97)
                    self.rankNumber = str(self.rank + 1)
                    self.coordinates = f"{self.fileCharacter}{self.rankNumber}"
                    if self.pieceToMove[0] is not None:
                        self.move = chess.Move.from_uci(f"{self.pieceToMove[1]}{self.coordinates}")
                        if self.move in self.board.legal_moves:
                            self.board.push(self.move)
                        self.piece = None
                        self.coordinates = None
                    self.pieceToMove = [self.piece, self.coordinates]
                    self.drawBoard()

    def drawBoard(self):
        """
        Draw a chessboard with the starting position and then redraw
        it for every new move.
        """
        self.arrowAfterMove = ()  # Having trouble right here
        self.kingInCheck = self.board.king(self.board.turn) if self.board.is_check() else None
        self.boardSvg = chess.svg.board(board=self.board,
                                        arrows=self.arrowAfterMove,
                                        check=self.kingInCheck)
        self.boardSvgEncoded = self.boardSvg.encode("UTF-8")
        self.drawBoardSvg = self.widgetSvg.load(self.boardSvgEncoded)

        return self.drawBoardSvg


if __name__ == "__main__":
    chessTitan = QApplication(sys.argv)
    window = MainWindow()
    window.show()
    chessTitan.exec()

issue_title: Slugifying Chinese characters with multiple readings
issue_body: This is probably beyond doable, since it would require extensive lookups, but slugifying can result in erroneous output if a given character has multiple readings (多音字), and context would be required to determine the correct slug, e.g. 行 is correctly rendered 'xing' when it stands alone, but 'hang' it would have to be slugified as 'hang' when it stands with 银行 (銀行) in a compound word. (See [3rd paragraph in this](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Chinese_characters#Function).)

issue_title: Password keys 1 and 2 are the same
issue_body:
self.pw and self.pw_scalar are the "same". They need to be cryptographically different and not dependent on the other:
```
self.pw_scalar = params.group.password_to_scalar(hkdf(pw, "pw1"))
self.pw = hkdf(pw, "pw2")
```
This is especially true for the client-server augmentation of SPAKE2, PAKE2+.
```
self.pw_scalar = params.group.password_to_scalar(hkdf(pw, "pw1"))
self.pw = hkdf(pw, "pw2")
self.pw3 = params.group.password_to_scalar(hkdf(pw, "pw3"))
```

issue_title: Jupyter HTML repr hangs on large collections
issue_body: Due to [this bug/behavior in ipython](https://github.com/ipython/ipython/issues/9771), `__repr__` and `_repr_html_` are called when a sequence is the last element evaluated in a jupyter notebook. Since `__repr__` prints a string of the entire collection so that `eval` can take it back in, this is extremely expensive while at the same time `_repr_html_` is written explicitly to give a summary view to avoid this problem.

issue_title: report client version to mailbox server
issue_body:
I've avoided doing this so far, but I think I need to have the client report its version to the mailbox/rendezvous server so I can measure new-release uptake. I don't currently have any visibility at all into what versions people are running, and I expect it'll help me plan out deprecation of old API versions if I knew what percentage of clients were capable of speaking the new versions.

My plan is to add a "version" key to the BIND message (which is the first message that clients send to the server upon each websocket connection). The value will be a 2-tuple of (implementation, version), with `("python", "0.10.5")` being the current value. The server will record (side, implementation, version, rounded_time) in the usage database, and tooling will de-duplicate `side` before aggregating the results (to avoid double-counting clients which lose their websocket connection and have to make a new one). (`side` is a random string, mostly used to avoid talking to ourselves, but also for deciding which side is the "leader" and which is the "follower" in the upcoming Dilation protocol).

issue_title: daemon mode
issue_body:
Suppose you're writing a frontend for the `wormhole` program, maybe in some other language than python (so it's not feasible to somehow link against python and import/use the `wormhole` library directly). It might be convenient if the program offered a daemon mode, so when you run `wormhole daemon` (instead of `wormhole send`, etc), it would start speaking some RPC protocol over stdin/stdout, and let the caller issue commands that create wormholes and send data through them.

(it might also be interesting to have the `wormhole daemon` program start listening on a localhost WebSocket, and take commands over that instead of stdin/stdout.. not sure)

I'm thinking an API that lets you create new wormholes (returning an identifier, used in later calls to distinguish which of multiple simultaneous wormholes you want to address), set or generate codes on them, initiate a connection, then send or receive data. The data `get()` function might be turned into a streaming thing (the app never calls `get()` directly, but rather every inbound wormhole message is asynchronously delivered over the socket as it arrives).

issue_title: Add tests for generic dataclasses
issue_body:
I think it makes sense to add tests that check that dataclasses interact nicely with ``typing.Generic`` and type variables. Most likely no changes is needed to the code (only tests) since we use a decorator ``@dataclass``. But still ``Generic`` uses a complex metaclass (this will be removed if/when PEP 560 is accepted and replaced with ``__init_subclass__`` and new friends).

I think it makes sense to add tests checking that things like this work:
```python
@dataclass
class LabeledBox(Generic[T]):
    content: T
    label: str = '<unknown>'

box = LabeledBox(42)
assert box.content == 42
assert box.label == '<unknown>'

boxes: List[LabeledBox[int]] = []  # subscripting the resulting class should work, etc.
```

issue_title: Idea: Unit tests for dataviz function
issue_body: I understand that it is difficult to write unit tests for dataviz functions because the output is visual. However, one possible way to write unit tests for dataviz functions is to provide the functions with fixed input, then take the output dataviz and save/hash/serialize it somehow. Then that saved/hashed/serialized can be compared to a known, correct saved/hashed/serialized output from before. That way the unit test will fire off if you change anything related to the plotting functionality.

issue_title: Runs dieing with AccessDeniedException on EFS DescribeFileSystems in us-west-2
issue_body:
The EFS service has [logic in place](https://github.com/jantman/awslimitchecker/blob/91780fd72098f70c5a27d78211ab7a79021f00e9/awslimitchecker/services/efs.py#L66-L73) to handle EndpointConnectionErrors from the [regions](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/general/latest/gr/rande.html#elasticfilesystem-region) that don't have EFS enabled:

```
        try:
            self._find_usage_filesystems()
        except EndpointConnectionError as ex:
            logger.warning(
                'Caught exception when trying to use EFS ('
                'perhaps the EFS service is not available in this '
                'region?): %s', ex
            )
```

This seemed to work fine up to February 22nd (4 days ago), logging messages for us-west-1 (where EFS isn't supported) like:

```
INFO:awslimitchecker.connectable:Connected to efs in region us-west-1
WARNING:awslimitchecker.services.efs:Caught exception when trying to use EFS (perhaps the EFS service is not available in this region?): Could not connect to the endpoint URL: "https://elasticfilesystem.us-west-1.amazonaws.com/2015-02-01/file-systems"
```

On the 23rd, in some of the accounts where we run this, the us-west-1 region began causing the entire awslimitchecker run to fail with a new error:

```
INFO:awslimitchecker.connectable:Connected to efs in region us-west-1
ERROR:__main__:Got unexpected (non-throttling) exception
Traceback (most recent call last):
... traceback omitted ...
ClientError: An error occurred (AccessDeniedException) when calling the DescribeFileSystems operation: This account does not have permission to access this service
```

The role this is running under clearly has the correct permissions, and is checking other regions without a problem. This seems to be a sudden change in behavior for the EFS API in us-west-1.

1. Figure out why this is happening for EFS and fix it.
2. Consider changing the way checks are executed, so that permissions errors for a single service result in an error message and eventually in a non-zero exit code, but don't prevent checking other services.

issue_title: Issues with start time
issue_body:
Running into some issues with the `start` parameter in `client.update()`. What we want is for the time to start from when the update is issued, however, that doesn't seem to be possible.

If set to `0`, the following error occurs:
```python
pypresence.exceptions.ServerError: Child "activity" fails because child "timestamps" fails
 because child "start" fails because "start" must be larger than or equal to 1
```
If set to `1`, the "start" time gets set to ~5 hours, which is definitely not what I expected. Example: [https://imgur.com/6vp9RMf](https://imgur.com/6vp9RMf)

Example code:
```python
from pypresence import Presence
import time

# PUT A TEST CLIENT ID HERE
client_id = ''

RPC = Presence(client_id)
RPC.connect()

start_time = 1
print(RPC.update(state="No important DMs please", details="Trying to sleep", start=start_time,
                 large_image="monoblobsleep", large_text="Sleeping. Please no DMs",

issue_title: Use sqlalchemy_utils.types.json.JSONType as TypeDecorator
issue_body:
Thanks for publishing this! I was about to write something similar when I found your blogpost. You saved me lots of time.

One quick improvement to your column types could be using the [JSONType from SQLAlchemy-Utils](https://sqlalchemy-utils.readthedocs.org/en/latest/data_types.html#module-sqlalchemy_utils.types.json) instead of doing your own implementation. It has native support for PostgreSQL's json column-type.

issue_title: benchmarking side-effectful code
issue_body:
I'm working on benchmarking some functions that modify their input. The input is a list of dictionaries, and the code sorts the list and also adds things to the dictionaries. As a result of this, the benchmarks are not totally true, because the first iteration modifies the input and the consequent iterations have to do much less work, because the input is already sorted. 

I can "fix" this problem by doing something (expensive) like deepcopy before the function I'm benchmarking is run, however, this will add to the running time statistics. Any thoughts?

issue_title: Add ability to change profiles (~/.ssh/config files)
issue_body:
I work in a large cloud infrastructure provider and I often work in environments where ip ranges overlap. DNS resolution is not always an option either.

It would be nice to be able to setup multiple assh.yml files (and corresponding assh.d dirs) and have a means of switching between them(environments). ie 'assh load dev-lab4-tenantE' to load an environment definition, 'assh load default' to load the default assh.yml config etc

Currently this can be done via 'ssh -F' but making it persistent till the next change would be ideal, a bit like a symlink or similar. This would save having to add a flag to the command every invocation. I've also tried aliasing the command but our environment runs into the hundreds so it becomes unwieldly managing aliases at that rate

Also adding the ability to visually identify the current context would be helpful.

I don't suppose this is a common requirement today but as more people continue down the path of overlay networks etc in the future I envisage it will be!

issue_title: Observes does not observe deleted objects
issue_body:
If observed property is a relationship, deleted objects are not observed. Example of functionality:
```
class Catalog(Base):
    __tablename__ = 'catalog'
    id = sa.Column(sa.Integer, primary_key=True)
    category_count = sa.Column(sa.Integer, default=0)
    categories = sa.orm.relationship('Category', backref='catalog')

    @observes('categories')
    def categories_observer(self, categories):
        print 'called'
        self.category_count = len(categories)



class Category(Base):
    __tablename__ = 'category'
    id = sa.Column(sa.Integer, primary_key=True)
    catalog_id = sa.Column(sa.Integer, sa.ForeignKey('catalog.id'))


category = Category()
category2 = Category()
catalog = Catalog(categories=[category, category2])
session.add(catalog)
session.commit()
print catalog.category_count  # 2

session.delete(category)
session.commit()
print catalog.category_count  # 2
```

Changing `@observes('categories')` to `@observes('categories.id')` changes last print to "1".

This is something related to `has_changes` as `has_changes(catalog, 'categories')` returns `False` although we deleted a category. That is not an error as `catalog.categories` still contains two items and is unaltered.

issue_title: Runtime detection of optimized module
issue_body:
Hi all,

I was wondering if it will be possible to detect at runtime if the imported module is the native python version or instead the "compiled" one using pythran. 

This is a nice to have in production system as well as during development stages. The rationale is detecting at runtime if the current system is running or not with the support of the optimized version of your code. 

Currently this is possible using a snippet like:
```python
import mymodule

if not mymodule.__file__.endswith('.so'):
    print('loaded .py module')
```

I'm not sure this is the way to go but I was wondering if it may be possible to add a set of globals to the generated module. This will allows the users to quickly check if the optimized version is available or not (e.g. __PYTHRAN) and to inspect some metadata regarding the compilation process (e.g compiler version, flags, timestamp and hash of the original .py file).  Several examples can be found for these flags. The hash one can provide a quick way to check if a given compile module still refer to actual version of its .py counterpart.

For instance as pseudocode:
```python
import hashlib

def check_source(mymodule):
    import mymodule

    if not mymodule.__file__.endswith('.so'):
        return False

    h = hashlib.sha1()
    
    with open('mymodule.py') as fp:
         h.update(fp.read())
    
    return mymodule.__PYTHRAN_HASH == h.hexdigest()
```

Then on the command line:
```
$ pythran --check mymodule.py
>> Compiled module (mymodule.so) matches (mymodule.py) source version.
```

issue_title: Works in Python2 but not in Python (Invalid Salt)
issue_body:
Hey there,

I am getting some odd behaviour in Python3.

My code looks like this:
```python
default_user = User(name="John Doe")
default_user.set_password("password")
assert default_user.check_password("password") is True
assert default_user.check_password("kldsjfsakdjf") is False
```

set_password is defined as:
```python
    def set_password(self, password):
        self.password = bcrypt.generate_password_hash(password)
```

```python
    def check_password(self, value):
        # Users without password can't log in
        # we prevent to hash an empty string
        if not value and not self.password:
            return False
        return bcrypt.check_password_hash(self.password, value)
```

This code works great in Python2.  When I run the same code in Python3 I get the "Invalid Salt" error.

Investigating it looks like instead of the hash being written to my db (Postgres) I get a different string.

For example in Python 3 when I look at User.password in the db I see:

```
\x24326224313324674b644e614e5570476d347143502e6539735063704f4e392e6a2e5956714b726a537575312e354e4c4971505761464e3771423843
```

instead of 

```python
b'$2b$13$gKdNaNUpGm4qCP.e9sPcpON9.j.YVqKrjSuu1.5NLIqPWaFN7qB8C'
```

which is the value before I save the user and the one I should receive when I check it. 

This looks like some kind of encoding error but I can't pinpoint what it is doing it!  Any suggestions would be greatly appreciated.  😄

issue_title: Follow-up CSRF question
issue_body:
I'm trying to understand how CSRF works when you follow the example in csrf_protection_with_cookies. I read the documentation you linked to an earlier CSRF question:

http://www.redotheweb.com/2015/11/09/api-security.html

That document says that CSRF protection happens because you're adding an additional token **to the request body**. Specifically, it says a server "should expect both a session cookie header, and a token in the response body.", and the example has the token in the JSON payload of a post.

In your csrf_protection_with_cookies example, I understand that the access_token is HTTP only, and the crsf_access_token is not, but if they're both sent with any request by the browser to the server as cookies, I'm not sure how CSRF protection is achieved?

I note that you also have examples of not sending the CSRF token as a cookie, but that certainly takes a bit more effort for the app developer. If the CSRF token as a cookie is effective, that would make my life easier, but I'm not sure how that could be the case.

To be more specific on why I'm confused, I've set up flask_jwt_extended following the example in crsf_protection_with_cookies. To test it, I'm trying an integration test that calls the server using the python requests library roughly as follows:

- Setting up a requests session object: `s = requests.session()`
- Calling the /login endpoint with a post to the session: `resp = s.post('/login', <login json>)`
- Calling a get method that is marked as protected with @jwt_required using the same session with get: `resp = s.get('/users')`. I would expect this get to fail unless I'd set the CSRF token in the 'X-CSRF-TOKEN' header as described in the comment in csrf_protection_with_cookies, but the request succeeds without any custom header set by me in the test code.

If a plain requests.get call works with @jwt_extended in this case, I must be missing how the CRSF protection works somehow. Shouldn't the request fail authentication because the X-CSRF-TOKEN header was not explicitly set in the get call?

issue_title: Squared euclidean distance breaks VPTree
issue_body:
Squared euclidean distance cannot be used in VPTree search and thus sqrt() should be calculated for result:
sqrt() should be used in https://github.com/DmitryUlyanov/Multicore-TSNE/blob/master/multicore_tsne/vptree.h#L71
or https://github.com/DmitryUlyanov/Multicore-TSNE/blob/master/multicore_tsne/vptree.h#L206

Using squared euclidean distance in VPTree causes search to not find all k nearest points.
See https://github.com/lvdmaaten/bhtsne/pull/41#issuecomment-248636146 and http://stevehanov.ca/blog/index.php?id=130

"It is worth repeating that you must use a distance metric that satisfies the triangle inequality. I spent a lot of time wondering why my VP tree was not working. It turns out that I had not bothered to find the square root in the distance calculation. This step is important to satisfy the requirements of a metric space, because if the straight line distance to a <= b+c, it does not necessarily follow that a2 <= b2 + c2."

Omitting sqrt in VPTree search seems to bring increased performance because it doesn't search all necessary branches in tree. You can ensure that by calculating t-SNE with both metrics and using same initial coordinates. You will see that output differs a bit. I have done this in comment https://github.com/lvdmaaten/bhtsne/pull/41#issuecomment-248827988

issue_title: Change application namespace to not clash with django-admin-sortable
issue_body:
I have a number of projects where I currently use django-admin-sortable, but would like to use django-admin-sortable2 because of its clean and lightweight approach to solving the sortable FK problem.

Unfortunately, because both libraries use the same package name: `adminsortable`, it is rather difficult if not impossible to use them side-by-side in the same project during transition. It also means that all of my migration history has to be either rewritten or discarded, because the existing migrations reference `adminsortable.SortableForeignKey`, which does not exist in this package.

I propose (and will submit a PR for shortly) that we change the application package name to 'adminsortable2'. This will have only the smallest implications to new users, but means many more people will be able to use this library.

issue_title: Allow all message fields to be overridden per message

Right now, not all fields can be overridden when sending a message.

For consistency and due to #10, all fields should be reviewed. The value from the record should have the highest priority, then comes the one from the class initializer. Falling back to standard fields in the record.

issue_title: Multiple queries?
The model does not have a model_attr 'language_code'
issue_body:
We are facing this problem with a translatable model that doesn't always have model attribute `language_code` after is fetched from the db.

The scenario is that we have setup celery_haystack to signal model changes to django_haystack to update a search index.

I looked at the source code of all 3 libraries to sort of understand what's happening and it seems that celery_haystack does a `get(pk=46914)` on the model manager. Some times language_code is indeed not available. It seems like a race condition, but I can't explain it since hvad's way of accessing cached_translations attributes is blocking?

I used a for loop to check if it exists up to 100 times, before accessing the attribute and it seems that it works most of the time now. But it's a non-deterministic solution I would like to avoid:

```python
class MyTranslatableModel(TranslatableModel):
    class Manager(TranslationManager):
        def get(self, *args, **kwargs):
            print '### GETTING MODEL ###'
            obj = super(MyTranslatableModel.Manager, self).get(*args, **kwargs)
            for i in range(100):
                if not hasattr(obj, 'language_code'):
                    print '### Attempt {0}: No language code ###'.format(i + 1)
                else:
                    break
            return obj
```

It becomes available at random iteration `i`.

Also here is the traceback from celery_haystack:

```
[2016-05-18 17:54:46,268: ERROR/Worker-1] celery_haystack.tasks.CeleryHaystackSignalHandler[2f876d00-7546-4886-8c7c-6c172c4e3dd9]: The model '<MyTranslatableModel: 46914>' does not have a model_attr 'language_code'.

    return self.convert(super(CharField, self).prepare(obj))

issue_title: Checking for case insensitive Content-Type
issue_body:
Hi, great library!

This line here checks for a case sensitive 'Content-Type':
https://github.com/siddhantgoel/streaming-form-data/blob/6fa2d23a3e5015a08144aa12903f206dc8cf49e7/streaming_form_data/parser.py#L10-L13

I was testing with curl and the browser, they both send the content type header as uppercase: 'CONTENT-TYPE', which causes this library to throw `ParseFailedException`. 

According to the http spec, headers are case-insensitive: https://stackoverflow.com/questions/7718476/are-http-headers-content-type-c-case-sensitive/7718542

It'd be great if you could make the dictionary `get` case insensitive.

issue_title: Metadata from field **kwargs is ignored
issue_body:
Marshmallow allows for any arbitrary kwargs to be added to the field in the `metadata` property - this is not respected for e.g. description and others (it is expected to be in a further key called `metadata` inside the `metadata` dict) - so specifying e.g. description directly on the field is not serialised out.

See: http://marshmallow.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api_reference.html?highlight=metadata#module-marshmallow.fields
