issue_title: upload_to doesn't affect upload location
issue_body:
When I specify `upload_to` as follows:

```
self.fields['summary'].widget = RedactorEditor(upload_to='summary')
```
The POST request correctly gets sent to `/redactor/upload/image/summary`, but the file itself gets placed in `REDACTOR_UPLOAD`, and the `upload_to` option seems to have no effect.

It looks like `urls.py` correctly handles the `upload_to` parameter, but digging into `form_valid` in `RedactorUploadView` class from `views.py` it looks like nothing is being done with it:

```
    def form_valid(self, form):
        file_ = form.cleaned_data['file']
        handler_class = import_class(self.upload_handler)
        uploader = handler_class(file_)
        uploader.save_file()
        ...
```

I think this should read:

```
    def form_valid(self, form):
        file_ = form.cleaned_data['file']
        handler_class = import_class(self.upload_handler)
        uploader = handler_class(file_,  upload_to=self.kwargs.get('upload_to', None))
        uploader.save_file()
        ...
```

I made the change locally and it now behaves as expected - if you need me to make a PR I can do so, but it's probably quicker for you to incorporate such a minor change in your own codebase.

Great plugin by the way - I researched a bunch of WYSIWYG editors for Django and this was by far the easiest to incorporate. Thanks!

issue_title: Specifying a start or end time of midnight for intraday_time_series causes a TypeError
issue_body:
If you call the `intraday_time_series` method and specify a start time or end time of midnight (i.e. 00:00), a TypeError is thrown. Minimum working example:

```
import fitbit
from datetime import time
from secrets import consumer_key, consumer_secret, user_key, user_secret

client = fitbit.Fitbit(consumer_key, 
                       consumer_secret, 
                       resource_owner_key=user_key, 
                       resource_owner_secret=user_secret)
client.intraday_time_series("activities/steps", start_time=time(0,0), end_time=(23,59))
```

This behaviour is caused by lines 376 and 379 of api.py, i.e.

```
if start_time and not end_time:
    raise TypeError("You must provide an end time when you provide a start time")

if end_time and not start_time:
    raise TypeError("You must provide a start time when you provide an end time")
```

The problem is that if `start_time = time(0,0)`, then it evaluates to `False` as a boolean (the same goes for `end_time`) so the check `if start_time and ...` will always evaluate to `False`. I think the idea here is to check that `start_time` is not equal to `None`, so the fix here should be pretty simple:

```
if start_time is not None and end_time is None:
    raise TypeError("You must provide an end time when you provide a start time")

if end_time is not None and start_time is None:
    raise TypeError("You must provide a start time when you provide an end time")
```

issue_title: Why no Codeowner for /cpp-package/?
issue_body:
Why isn't there a code owner for  `/cpp-package/` in [CODEOWNERS](https://github.com/apache/incubator-mxnet/blob/master/CODEOWNERS)?
```
# Language bindings
/R-package/       @thirdwing
/scala-package/   @yzhliu
/perl-package/    @sergeykolychev
/python/          @szha
```
It seems like it would be @lx75249 because of [Integrate cpp package #5251] and it looks like @lx75249 gets asked to review in PRs to `/cpp-package/` e.g. #9220 and #7495. Having a code owner is important PRs since some PRs can't be merged without having someone who is familiar with the package to review even someone else actually reviews the code and all the code-owner has to do is write LGTM.

## Minimum reproducible example

```python
import mxnet as mx
import random
import sys

#parameter to break this code or not
break_code = sys.argv[1].lower() == "true"

#synthetically create 1000 encoded sentences
encoded_sentences = []
for i in list(range(1000)):
    sentence_length = random.randint(1, 20)
    sentence = random.sample(range(20), sentence_length)
    encoded_sentences.append(sentence)

#define hyperparameters/info
vocab = list(set(index for list in encoded_sentences for index in list))
batch_size = 18
num_embed = 10
num_hidden = 3
buckets = [5, 10, 15, 20]
epochs = 15

# Create and register a custom initializer
@mx.init.register
class WeightInit(mx.init.Initializer):
    def __init__(self):
        super(WeightInit, self).__init__()
    def _init_weight(self, _, arr):
        #set all weights to 1
        arr[:] = 1
        #scale the weight for not entity values
        arr[:,5,:] /= 5
    def _init_bias(self, _, arr):
        arr[:] =1

#use mxnet bucketing iterator, label is next value in sentence
train_iter = mx.rnn.BucketSentenceIter(sentences=encoded_sentences,
                                       batch_size=batch_size,
                                       buckets=buckets,
                                       data_name='data',
                                       label_name='softmax_label')

#define recurrent cell
r_cell = mx.rnn.LSTMCell(num_hidden=num_hidden)

#architecture is defined in a function, to allow variable length input sequences
def sym_gen(seq_len):
    """function that creates a network graph, depending on sequence length"""

    #define data shapes
    data_shape = (batch_size, seq_len)
    label_shape = (batch_size, seq_len)

    data = mx.sym.Variable('data')
    print("\ndata shape: ", data.infer_shape(data=data_shape)[1][0], "\n")

    label = mx.sym.Variable('softmax_label')
    print("\nlabel shape: ", label.infer_shape(softmax_label=label_shape)[1][0], "\n")

    #initialize weight array for use in our loss function, using a custom initializer and prevent the weights from being updated
    label_weights = mx.sym.BlockGrad(mx.sym.Variable(shape=(1, len(vocab), 1), init=WeightInit(), name='label_weights'),name = "blocked_weights")
    broadcast_label_weights = mx.sym.broadcast_to(label_weights, shape = (batch_size, len(vocab), seq_len), name = 'broadcast_weights')
    print("\ninput weights shape: ", broadcast_label_weights.infer_shape()[1][0])

    embed = mx.sym.Embedding(data, input_dim=len(vocab), output_dim=num_embed,name='embed')
    print("\nembed layer shape: ", embed.infer_shape(data=data_shape)[1][0], "\n")

    output, shapes = r_cell.unroll(seq_len, inputs=embed, merge_outputs=True)
    print("\nconcatenated recurrent layer shape: ", output.infer_shape(data=data_shape)[1][0], "after ", seq_len, " unrolls\n")

    reshape = mx.sym.Reshape(output, shape=(-1, num_hidden))
    print("\nafter reshaping: ", reshape.infer_shape(data=data_shape)[1][0], "\n")

    fc = mx.sym.FullyConnected(reshape, num_hidden=len(vocab), name='pred')
    print("\nfully connected layer shape: ", fc.infer_shape(data=data_shape)[1][0], "\n")

    label = mx.sym.Reshape(label, shape=(-1,))
    print("\nlabel shape after reshaping: ", label.infer_shape(softmax_label=label_shape)[1][0], "\n")

    onehot_label = mx.sym.one_hot(indices=label, depth=len(vocab), name='one_hot_labels')
    print("\none hot label shape: ", onehot_label.infer_shape(softmax_label=label_shape)[1][0], "\n")

    sm = mx.sym.softmax(fc, name='predicted_proba')
    print("\nsoftmax shape: ", sm.infer_shape(data=data_shape)[1][0], "\n")

    if break_code == False:
        loss = -((onehot_label * mx.sym.log(sm)) + ((1 - onehot_label) * mx.sym.log(1 - sm)))
    else:
        loss = -((onehot_label * mx.sym.log(sm)) + ((1 - onehot_label) * mx.sym.log(1 - sm))) * broadcast_label_weights

    print("\ncross entropy loss shape: ", loss.infer_shape(data = data_shape, softmax_label=label_shape)[1][0])

    loss_grad = mx.sym.MakeLoss(loss, name = 'loss')
    print("\nloss grad shape: ", loss_grad.infer_shape(data=data_shape, softmax_label=label_shape)[1][0])

    return loss_grad, ('data',), ('softmax_label',)

#create a trainable bucketing module on cpu
model = mx.mod.BucketingModule(sym_gen=sym_gen, default_bucket_key=train_iter.default_bucket_key, context=mx.cpu())

#fit the module
metric = mx.metric.create('loss')
model.bind(data_shapes=train_iter.provide_data,
           label_shapes=train_iter.provide_label)
model.init_params()
model.init_optimizer(optimizer='Adam')
for epoch in range(epochs):
    train_iter.reset()
    metric.reset()
    for batch in train_iter:
        model.forward(batch, is_train=True)             # compute predictions
        model.backward()                                # compute gradients
        model.update()                                  # update parameters
        model.update_metric(metric, batch.label)        # update metric
    print('\n', 'Epoch %d, Training %s' % (epoch, metric.get()))
```

## Steps to reproduce/solve

1. `python mwe.py false` to train successfully
2. `python mwe.py true` to reproduce error

issue_title: MXNet MKLDNN build dependency/flow discussion
issue_body:
Hi MXNet owners,

  This is Jin from Intel MXNet Shanghai team, we spent some time analyzing current MXNet make/cmake flow, especially the part related with MKLDNN and BLAS selection and full MKL/MKLML library link usage/dependency. We came out with a small summary slides mainly talk about below items:

(1) MKLDNN, full MKL, MKLML library relationship
(2) BLAS selection
(3) Our preferred MXNet Build Logic regarding full MKL/MKLML library
(4) Current build logic, issues, and potential improvements

  Please help to take at the slides and we can discuss for further comments. Thanks.

issue_title: Spark Model Training with multiple executors
issue_body:
This is more of a feature request that I may even be willing to work on in my spare time. However, I first wanted to see if anyone is either working on this or had any specific plans? Basically right now, it seems like you can only use one executor for training an mxnet model. 

It would be nice if mxnet could do model parameter averaging across spark partitions over each iteration of training (or multiple). This would require each partition to have access to an mxnet model, then apply the training on the subset of data (partition), and after an iteration(s) is completed, we could then average the weights and biases for the central core model. We then would pass the updated model to each of the partitions for another iteration or series of iterations and repeat. 

I know there are other deep learning libraries that have something similar, and I wanted to see where you guys were on this.

issue_title: It's quite hard to embed a YouTube video
issue_body:
The steps required for an end-user to actually embed a YouTube video seem quite hard:

- Find the video on YouTube
- Click on 'share'
- Go to the 'embed' tab
- Extract the 'src' attribute from the generated iframe code

Especially the last step is in my opinion way too hard for the average user.

My suggestion would be to either extract the src attribute if the whole iframe code is pasted into the embed field, or even better, to accept the URL of the video (in the format https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=XXXXXXXXXXX) and convert that to an embed link.

If I remember correctly, Django Embed Video uses the last option, which IMHO greatly enhances usability.

issue_title: Release 2.0.10 still reports sops 2.0.9 (latest)
`directory_regex` support (or something similar)?
issue_body:
I have users of our SOPS implementation that would prefer to use subdirectories to determine what encryption keys to use.  Our project structure is as follows:

```
├── engineering
├── ops
├── resources
└── ssl
```

Right now, we have a .sops.yaml file in each directory and require users to cd to the directory before creating a new file.  I get a lot of forgetful users who will create secrets from the top level.

So instead of

```
cd engineering
sops foo
```

we get

```
sops engineering/foo
```

If I could extend the configuration featureset to include a `directory_regex` I can run a regex on the first filename that is checked and see if it matches.  If no match, pass through to the `filename_regex` as it works today.

```
creation_rules:
  - directory_regex: ^engineering/
    kms: '...'
    pgp: '...'
  - directory_regex: ^ops/
    kms: '...'
    pgp: '...'
```

happy to submit a patch if you are interested in accepting

The DeepSpeech paper[[1](https://arxiv.org/abs/1412.5567)] indicates that they maximized
```
Q(c) = log(P_n(c|x)) + a·log(P_l(c)) +   b·word_count(c)
```
where `a` and `b` are hyperparameters, `P_n(c|x)` is the probability of the string `c` given the wav files `x`, while `P_l(c)` is the probability of the string `c` according to the language model, and `word_count(c)` is the number of words in `c`.

To adjust the hyperparameters `a` and `b` we can use the serialized tuples `(wavID, resultText, logProbability)` then adjust `a` and `b` offline to maximize `Q(c)`. This should be a simple machine learning model in which the parameters `a` and `b` are the only things that need to be learned and the data set is the serialized tuples.

Note: In the tuple it's obvious what `resultText` and `logProbability` are; `wavID` is less obvious. However, it's fine to have `wavID` be the index of the wav file in the epoch.

Verify that merge_repeated=False is the correct thing to do

To fix issue #53, we added merged_repeated=False to the ctc_beam_search_decoder call. This allows it to return repeated letters and allows us to get to WER=0 for the ldc93s1 corpus.

I am uncertain if this was the correct change to make globally, however, and am concerned that it only worked on ldc93s1 due to the particular properties of that single audio file. It's probably fine, but opening this issue to keep track of it. If we have some unexplained behaviour with poor WER and we see repeated letters, we may want to investigate this.

issue_title: Can't support the same query parameter twice in one URL
issue_body:
#### Issue Summary

As far as I can tell, this library doesn't let you make requests to URLs which use the same query parameter key more than once, such as `http://example.com?bar=foo&bar=baz`. This is a problem because many HTTP APIs use this pattern, including SendGrid's own v3 endpoint `/subusers/reputations`. It might be good if you could pass either a dict or a list of two-tuples.

#### Steps to Reproduce

Look at python_http_client.client.Client._build_url. `query_params` is expected to be a dict, or at least something on which you can call `items`.

#### Technical details:

* python-http-client Version: discovered on 1.2.3 but looks like it's applicable to master as well (latest commit: master)
* Python Version: 3.6

issue_title: Session.close() fails when using transactions in some cases
issue_body:
I have this test code. Since the docs stress that the session should be closed whatever happens, I have an session.close() in the try/except/finally construct. 

There seems to be a difference between an exception in cypher (error case 1) and a regular exception (see error case 2).  In the first case I get an error in the line session.close(): "neo4j.v1.exceptions.CypherError: rollback cannot be done when there is no open transaction in the session.” In the second case (of course commenting out case 1): no error and the tx is rolled back correctly.


```
from neo4j.v1 import GraphDatabase, basic_auth, TRUST_ON_FIRST_USE

driver = GraphDatabase.driver("bolt://localhost:7000",
                              auth=basic_auth("neo4j", "test"),
                              encrypted=False,
                              trust=TRUST_ON_FIRST_USE)

session = driver.session()
tx = session.begin_transaction()

try:
    result = tx.run("CREATE (n:Person{lastname:'test', firstname:'T'})")
    # Error case 1: with the following 2 lines uncommentend one gets the problem
    result = tx.run("XXMATCH (n:Person)-[r]->(m) RETURN n, r, m")
    result = list(result)
    # Error case 2: with the following line uncommented instead of the previous two one does not get the problem
    # raise Exception('Testing exception')

    tx.commit()
except Exception as e:
    print('*** ',e)
    tx.rollback()
finally:
    session.close()
```

issue_title: HiveQueryTask doesn't use output/isn't atomic
issue_body:
`HiveQueryTask` assumes that the user will enter an output path/partition/table directly in the query. If you specify an `output()` anyways (which you still want to, to help the scheduler), and then reference the same output in the hive query the run operation will not be atomic since Hive will directly write to that directory. Since we want to try to keep as much atomic things as possible I think the HiveQueryTask should by default add a temporary output path to the query and then move that result into a final destination once the run has completed successfully.
That way we can get much cleaner queries as well, especially if we do output-type aware insertions, i.e. doing INSERT OVERWRITE DIRECTORY if output is an `HdfsTarget`, doing LOCAL DIRECTORY if it's a `LocalTarget` and doing table/partition creation if output is a `HiveTableTarget` or `HivePartitionTarget`. I think that would create a much smoother workflow for working with scheduled Hive queries.

issue_title: Don't use hard-coded tmp_dir, use tempfile.mkdtemp
issue_body:
For your consideration:

Running luigi-based hadoop jobs on a system by more than one user inevitably causes problems.  Instead of using a configurable location to place temporary directories, it's best practices to use the built-in facilities.  In this case, that means using tempfiles.mkdtemp.


```diff
diff --git a/luigi/hadoop.py b/luigi/hadoop.py
index de654cd..6b58afc 100644
--- a/luigi/hadoop.py
+++ b/luigi/hadoop.py
@@ -297,10 +297,8 @@ class HadoopJobRunner(JobRunner):
         if runner_path.endswith("pyc"):
             runner_path = runner_path[:-3] + "py"
 
-        base_tmp_dir = configuration.get_config().get('core', 'tmp-dir', '/tmp/luigi')
-        self.tmp_dir = os.path.join(base_tmp_dir, 'hadoop_job_%016x' % random.getrandbits(64))
+        self.tmp_dir = tempfile.mkdtemp()
         logger.debug("Tmp dir: %s", self.tmp_dir)
-        os.makedirs(self.tmp_dir)
 
         # build arguments
         map_cmd = 'python mrrunner.py map'
@@ -381,6 +379,7 @@ class HadoopJobRunner(JobRunner):
         self.finish()
 
     def finish(self):
+        # FIXME: check for isdir?
         if self.tmp_dir and os.path.exists(self.tmp_dir):
             logger.debug('Removing directory %s', self.tmp_dir)
             shutil.rmtree(self.tmp_dir)
```

issue_title: Process messages asynchronously with multiprocessing pool
issue_body:
Given a single stdio language server is responsible for a single workspace we may end up running into performance issues, particularly around cheap requests being blocked being more expensive requests.

Unfortunately, Jedi is not thread safe because it relies heavily on global state for caching. This is unfortunate as most of our non-jedi providers are io-bound (spawn subprocesses) and would work quite well with python threading, even with the GIL. 

Instead it might be worth having a multiprocessing pool of Jedi workers each with their own cache and a results queue that is consumed and written back to the client.

To make things easy, if we already have a multiprocessing results queue we may as well use multiprocessing for everything.

issue_title: How to set a null value in patch?
Support offset/limit pagination strategy
issue_body:
Currently used `page[number]`/`page[size]` pagination strategy is less flexible than `offset`/`limit` strategy. Getting page by `number` and `size` is easy and efficient with `offset`/`limit`, but getting data given offset and limit can be quite inefficient if we can only set page.

For example, the worst case. We want to get `x` rows with offset `x - 1`. We need to get `2x - 1` rows and cut requited locally. If we were using `offset`/`limit` pagination strategy we would have downloaded almost twice less data from our server.

It would be better if `flask-rest-jsonapi` supported both strategies.

issue_title: Submission thread seems to block on unrelated tags
issue_body:
I'm working in some of the future_tag code, and I'm seeing (https://github.com/boto/s3transfer/blob/develop/s3transfer/futures.py#L328)

```python

        with self._lock:
            # Wait if there is a maximum reached
            self._wait_for_futures_to_complete_if_needed(tag)
            # Submit the task to the executor
            future = self._executor.submit(fn, *args, **kwargs)
            # Add the recently added future to the currently running futures
            self._add_future_to_currently_running(future, tag)
        return future
```

So suppose I have a max tags of `{"A": 1, "B": 1}` and sequence such as:

```
T1: submit tag A --> noblock
T1: submit tag A --> block on waiting futures
T2: submit tag B --> block on lock acquire
```

Where T1/T2 and A/B are tags.  This means that even though T2 is submitting tag B which has no futures, the submission on tag B will still block on the `with self._lock` until a future for tag A completes.

```
        processor = no_process

    # Only process the query once instead of for every choice
    if scorer in [fuzz.UWRatio, fuzz.UQRatio]:
        pre_processor = partial(utils.full_process, force_ascii=False)
        scorer = partial(scorer, full_process=False)
    elif scorer in [fuzz.WRatio, fuzz.QRatio,
                    fuzz.token_set_ratio, fuzz.token_sort_ratio,
                    fuzz.partial_token_set_ratio, fuzz.partial_token_sort_ratio]:
        pre_processor = partial(utils.full_process, force_ascii=True)
        scorer = partial(scorer, full_process=False)
    else:
        pre_processor = no_process
    processed_query = pre_processor(processed_query)
    count = -1
    try:
        # See if choices is a dictionary-like object.
        for key, choice in choices.items():
            count = count + 1
            processed = pre_processor(processor(choice))
            score = scorer(processed_query, processed)
            if score >= score_cutoff:
                yield (choice, score, key,count)
    except AttributeError:
        # It's a list; just iterate over it.
        for choice in choices:
            count = count + 1
            processed = pre_processor(processor(choice))
            score = scorer(processed_query, processed)

            if score >= score_cutoff:
                yield (choice, score,count)


def extract(query, choices, processor=default_processor, scorer=default_scorer, limit=5):
    sl = extractWithoutOrder(query, choices, processor, scorer)
    return heapq.nlargest(limit, sl, key=lambda i: i[1]) if limit is not None else \
        sorted(sl, key=lambda i: i[1], reverse=True)
```
When I tried to implement it, the result was what it was previously showing by fuzzywuzzy.

```
import process as p
box=['ness', 'apple','banana','carrot','duck','eagle','fish','gate','hitler']
p.extract('b',box)
[('banana', 90), ('apple', 0), ('carrot', 0), ('duck', 0), ('eagle', 0)]
```
But what I am expecting it to return is:

`[('banana', 90, 2), ('apple', 0, 1), ('carrot', 0, 3), ('duck', 0, 4), ('eagle', 0, 5)]`

Kindly let me know the suggestion.

issue_body:
```
$ env/bin/nosetests --with-xunit --with-xcoverage --cover-min-percentage=100

------------------------------------------------------------
colander                         989      2    99%   2207-2208

------------------------------------------------------------
TOTAL                           3934      2    99%   
nose.plugins.cover: ERROR: TOTAL Coverage did not reach minimum required: 100%
```

The code in question is:
```python
        try:
            functools.update_wrapper(self, wrapped)
        except AttributeError: #non-function
            self.__doc__ = getattr(wrapped, '__doc__', None)
```

And the reason can be [found in the docs](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functools.html#functools.update_wrapper):
> Changed in version 3.2: Missing attributes no longer trigger an AttributeError.

issue_title: Wrong mode mask when adding files to the index
issue_body:
0fdf6c3aaff49494c47aaeb0caa04b3016e10a26 changed the mode mask from `0o111` to `0o100`. We're encountering issues when committing files marked as executable:

After committing such a file with GitPython, our repo shows the following behavior:
```
# git status
On branch develop
nothing to commit, working directory clean
```
`git status` shows everything as clean (as it should be).

But if we `git stash` the changes:
```
# git stash
error: feeding unmodified <file> to diffcore
error: feeding unmodified <file> to diffcore
Saved working directory and index state WIP on develop: 6cdd426 ... (10136)
HEAD is now at 6cdd426 ... (10136)
```

And the stash turns up empty:
```
# git stash show
```

This is an issue for us because we end up in a dirty repo and can't execute some git commands anymore without resetting/stashing some inexistent changes.

I don't know what the rational behind the `0o100` mask is, but `0o111` works for our use case.

issue_title: git-add doesn't seem to work on submodules
issue_body:
It seems that `index.add` doesn't work with submodules. Found exactly same issue/question on [stackoverflow](http://stackoverflow.com/questions/31835812/gitpython-how-to-commit-updated-submodule).

Reproduction
---------

First, some shell:
```bash
git init master_repo
cd master_repo
echo "First" > progress_simulator
git add progress_simulator
git commit -m 'First commit'
git submodule add -b master git@github.com:gitpython-developers/GitPython.git subrepo
git commit -m 'Second commit: subrepo'
```

Now, the Python:
```python
import git
master_repo = git.Repo('.')
[subrepo_sm] = master_repo.submodules
subrepo = git.Repo(subrepo_sm.path)
with open('subrepo/interference.txt', 'w'): pass
subrepo.index.add(['interference.txt'])
subrepo.index.commit('My intrusion into this project')
master_repo.index.add([subrepo_sm])
```

Expected vs result
-------

I expected to see `master_repo` index acknowledge that the `subrepo` submodule pointer changed. However, nothing happens.

System info
-----

```bash
$ python -V
Python 3.4.3

$ git --version
git version 2.4.2
```

issue_title: `git status` output has changed in git 1.8.5
issue_body: In git versions > 1.8.4, the output of *git status* has changed such that the lines no longer have a "#" comment prefix by default. (See status.displayCommentPrefix in http://git-scm.com/docs/git-config) As a result, Repo.untracked_files() no longer parses the lines correctly. It seems that the --porcelain option to *git status* is specifically designed for this type of use.

issue_body:
For the keyword "Capture Page Screenshot"'s documentation, it uses the keyword "Should Be Equal" to imply that certain files should be equal. However, the keyword cannot actually be used to compare images or screenshots (it simply uses the arguments as strings in these cases and fails). 

I think the documentation should be clarified by noting that "Should Be Equal" in this case would not work, or remove the keyword from the example altogether.

issue_title: pipenv install fails on proprietary package not available on pypi
issue_body:
I would like to install a company proprietary internal package that is shipped with the project. Is only available in a folder relative to the project root and it's absolute path may vary between environments.

This is at least connected to #958 and #914

##### Expected result

That the package `multiinfo` would be installed and put in the Pipfile in a way that can be used on production environment.

1. Create a non-published not generally available python package,
2. Create a dist `python setup.py bdist_wheel` 
3. Install resulting wheel with pipenv

issue_title: Question - how to build falcon with cython using pipenv
issue_body:
This is a cross post from stack overflow - https://stackoverflow.com/questions/48805350/how-to-build-falcon-with-cython-using-pipenv
In the  [falcon docs](http://falcon.readthedocs.io/en/stable/user/install.html) it is mentioned that in order to build falcon with cython the following commands need to be issued:
>$ pip install cython

>$ pip install --no-binary :all: falcon

The question is how to do this with pipenv.

First of all i want to say Pipfile that i have build dependency cython. 

I want also to pass the parameters --no-binary :all: falcon to pip.

How can i accomplish this with pipenv?

issue_title: Will pipenv have an better shortname in the future?
issue_body:
`pipenv` is not easy to type. I usally get typo when use this command. And It is not easy to pronounce either.

It mix two type of naming scheme, `pip` in short for `Python Package Index` and `env` is the prefix of `Environment`. 

Dose anyone think `pipe` or `pie` is a better shortname?

issue_title: pipenv install raises error
issue_body:
Be sure to check the existing issues, both open and closed.

Describe the issue briefly here.

When I attempt to run `pipenv install -r requirements.txt` I get an error.

##### Describe your environment

1. OS Type: Max OSX
1. Python version: `$ python -V`: Python 3.6.4
1. Pipenv version: `$ pipenv --version`: pipenv, version 11.1.4

##### Expected result

Describe what you expected.

I expected to be able to install my packages.

##### Actual result

When possible, provide the verbose output (`--verbose`), especially for locking and dependencies resolving issues.

When I attempt to run `pipenv install -r requirements.txt` I get the following error:

issue_title: Ignores nested install_requires of setuptools
issue_body:
I have the following dependency structure:

```
ansible=2.1.0.0 -> paramiko -> cryptography -> setuptools>=11.3
```

In `ansible setup.py`:
```
install_requires = [setuptools, ...]
```

in `cryptography setup.py`:
```
install_requires = [setuptools>=11.3, ...]
```

The **good** case:
I create a venv. By default it uses `setuptools==2.2`. A pip install of paramiko properly upgrades setuptools, because of cryptography's version dependency.

The **bad** case:
I create another venv. Same default version `setuptools==2.2`. A pip install of ansible does not upgrade my setuptools. It **ignores** cryptography's setuptools dependency.

The catch. If I modify ansible's setup.py and remove its dependency of `setuptools` (currently any version) then setuptools gets updated to match cryptography's version dependency.

issue_title: Disable interpolation on config file parsing on Python 3
issue_body: In [Python 20754](http://bugs.python.org/issue20754), we've observed that interpolation was inadvertently turned on for setup.cfg parsing in the update to Python 3. A patch is being drafted to fix the issue for bugfix versions of Python 3.5 and 3.6 and for Python 3.7, but the issue will persist for older Pythons. Setuptools can provide forward compatibility.

issue_title: execsitecustomize in site.py doesn't clear exceptions
issue_body: execsitecustomize in site.py, also execusersitecustomize, are somewhat outdated that they only silence ImportError. They now in virtualenv are just what they are in Python2.5. In Python2.5, although they don't catch exceptions, but initsite in pythonrun.c clears all exceptions. So any error in sitecustomize.py won't block the process to run. But in Python2.6+, initsite no longer clears exceptions, exceptions are matched in site.py, so exceptions raised in sitecustomize.py still won't block the process to run. But with virtualenv, new interpreters and old site.py means the process dies immediately at start if there is any error in site customize.py. This behavior is not consistent with system one.

issue_title: Compiled regular expressions cache is exhausted by language detection
issue_body:
The `re` module's cache of compiled regular expressions is exhausted by the language detection when fails to detect a language, causing the same regular expressions to be compiled again each time an invalid date is passed.
```
>>> import timeit
>>> from dateparser import DateDataParser
>>> ddp = DateDataParser(allow_redetect_language=True)
>>> timeit.timeit(lambda: ddp.get_date_data("not really a date"), number=100)
13.68727993965149
```

Increasing `re._MAXCACHE` prevents the cache from being cleared and all the patterns from being compiled again on each attempt.
```
>>> import timeit
>>> from dateparser import DateDataParser
>>> ddp = DateDataParser(allow_redetect_language=True)
>>> import re
>>> re._MAXCACHE = float('inf')
>>> timeit.timeit(lambda: ddp.get_date_data("not really a date"), number=100)
3.077785015106201
```

Profiling shows that most time is spent in `re.sub()` called by `Language._simplify()` and `re.compile()` called by `Dictionary._construct_split_regex()`.

issue_title: Support transports without ports
issue_body:
Currently, Klein calls `request.getHost().port` (see https://github.com/twisted/klein/blob/master/src/klein/resource.py#L77-L81 ) to get the port number to add to the server name. Unfortunately, if the `IAddress` returned by `request.getHost()` doesn't have a `port` attribute (if it's from a loopback transport or a unix socket, for example) it breaks.

A possible solution is to check for a `port` attribute or catch an `AttributeError` instead of just assuming it's there. :-)

issue_title: Unbounded HistoryScreen?
issue_body:
Hi everyone!

I've been thinking it would be nice to extend HistoryScreen so it can have an unbounded history size. I believe the history parameter in HistoryScreen.__init__() is used to set the maxlen of the deques that manage the history buffers. Could we add a special case so that maxlen is not set, allowing the top and bottom histories to grow without bound?

Also, has anyone thought of implementing a "sliding window" approach to making a scrollback buffer?

I can see it working something like this:

Console output is stored in a single buffer, which stores all input fed to the terminal emulator. New lines are added to the end of the buffer, pretty much as is done now. 

Pagination would done via "views". A view would represent a distinct region of the output buffer, with a height equal to the size of the terminal. If the user wants to "page up", the start can be changed by the height of the terminal, which would change it's position in the output buffer. To get what's on screen you'd just return the lines in the output buffer that are within the screen view area (e.g. output_buffer[view_start:view_end])

I know this would probably be a big architectural change, but i think it has the potential to make things a lot simpler and reduce the amount of time spent shuffling strings around. We could also potentially use a feature like that to make it super easy to generate transcripts of sessions in the terminal emulator as well.

Thoughts?

issue_title: "Python's standard datetime object is broken"
issue_body:
This is a documentation issue, but I figure it may be best to talk to developers. 

https://segment.com/docs/libraries/python/#timezones-in-python

Python datetime objects are not "broken". The Python datetime module, in fact, is one of the best standard library implementations of all the programming languages I've worked with. The Python core devs knew timezones were [difficult to work with](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=-5wpm-gesOY). Libraries that deal with timezones are gnarly messes of spaghetti code that are constantly updated. Most people use `pytz`, which is able to be versioned independently of your installed Python, instead of being locked into timezones that existed at the date your version of Python was released.

The core team abstracted the timezone madness into the `tzinfo` classes, which you can implement to determine the offsets for a particular date.

`tzinfo` classes are not required to use datetime objects in Python, which is good. It allows you to work with dates in an abstract way. These are called "naive" datetime objects. datetime objects which do have `tzinfo` objects are called "aware" datetime objects. This is documented in the second paragraph of the [datetime module docs](https://docs.python.org/3.5/library/datetime.html).

The following sentence on your docs shows a misunderstanding of how Python datetime objects work.

![image](https://cloud.githubusercontent.com/assets/236501/14128074/03e1c6ca-f5e3-11e5-976d-8131ad766abd.png)

They don't "lose timezone information", whatever that means. You're not giving timezone information to the `now()` function in the first place. Try this: `datetime.datetime.now(tz=pytz.utc)`.

The following image on your docs depict the behavior of naive datetime objects in a way as if you're expecting them to be timezone aware. `now()` doesn't create timezone aware datetime objects unless you pass the `tz` keyword.

![image](https://cloud.githubusercontent.com/assets/236501/14127660/3e12d20c-f5df-11e5-9483-21302fbe6ee4.png)

All you need to do, as a library, is require timezone aware datetime objects. Then you can `isoformat` them and all will be fine.

Please fix the documentation to not hate on Python datetime objects because of a misunderstanding.

issue_title: FluentSender suppresses too many exceptions
issue_body:
In _send_internal(), all exceptions are captured and suppressed, even if they are the result of a basic configuration error. This results in a total, silent failure of the library. 

In my case, I inadvertently set the port number to a string, rather than an int. Fluentd failed silently.

Ultimately, I modified the code to log the exception in _send_internal, and received:

```
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "/usr/local/lib/python3.5/site-packages/fluent/sender.py", line 97, in _send_internal
    self._reconnect()
  File "/usr/local/lib/python3.5/site-packages/fluent/sender.py", line 124, in _reconnect
    sock.connect((self.host, self.port))
TypeError: an integer is required (got type str)
```

I would suggest:
1. Add basic type checking to the constructor.
2. Revise the overly broad exception handling to only catch transient connection errors, allowing other exceptions to be handled appropriately by the calling code.

issue_title: DOCS: Direct user questions to https://forum.image.sc (add link to project README)
issue_body:
## Description
Now that the Scientific Community Image Forum exists at https://forum.image.sc/, scikit-image should have a link to it in the README (a good first issue!)

Very likely this also means we should remove the StackOverflow badge & link, so there is one clear preferred path for questions. That will probably mean less duplication (see [#1](https://forum.image.sc/t/image-gets-rescaling-after-normalize-using-opencv-and-scikit-image/19065), [#2](https://stackoverflow.com/questions/51850688/image-gets-rescaling-after-normalize-using-opencv-and-scikit-image), [#3](https://github.com/scikit-image/scikit-image/issues/3352)) for both question askers and answerers.
It would be a step towards alignment with the Scientific Community Image Forum [policy discussed here](https://forum.image.sc/t/formal-policy-for-project-to-be-a-software-partner/18834):
> A) the organization links to the forum in its documentation regarding how users should seek support and/or discussion; and 
> B) it does not promote additional, separate, project-specific discussion channels more prominently than this forum.

issue_title: Remove duplicate mean of template calculation in skimage.feature.match_template
issue_body:
Reviewing `match_template` I found 

    template_volume = np.prod(template.shape)
    template_ssd = np.sum((template - template.mean()) ** 2)

    if image.ndim == 2:
        xcorr = fftconvolve(image, template[::-1, ::-1],
                            mode="valid")[1:-1, 1:-1]
    elif image.ndim == 3:
        xcorr = fftconvolve(image, template[::-1, ::-1, ::-1],
                            mode="valid")[1:-1, 1:-1, 1:-1]

    nom = xcorr - image_window_sum * (template.sum() / template_volume)

I.e. `template.mean()` is calculated once, and then further down `template.sum() / template_volume`; presumably we can make this faster using e.g. 

    template_mean = template.mean()

and using that both times it's needed?

Currently, our Freeimage TIFF reader just reads the first plane of a multipage tiff and calls it a day. It would be good to figure out how to support multipage tiff properly. (Though tifffile handles it admirably, users might set freeimage for whatever reason, and be stuck with buggy code.)

See #1440 for a related issue.

issue_title: Gaussian filter does not have default value for sigma
issue_body:
Most functions of ``scikit-image`` have only one non-keyword argument, which is an input image, and all other arguments are optional keyword arguments. I would suggest that we make the ``sigma`` parameter of ``filters.gaussian`` optional (eg with the default value of 1), for more consistency between functions. In addition, having only one non-kw parameter, which is an image, makes introspection and testing easier.

What do you think?

issue_title: SDR Classifier and region don't handle multiple calls correctly
issue_body:
The SDR classifier updates `_patternNZHistory` each time `compute` is called. But clients are calling `compute` multiple times per record. This is because there are sometimes multiple categories to learn for each record.

We should support this either by allowing multiple categories to be specified to `compute` or by enabling multiple calls to `compute` with only a single update to `_patternNZHistory`.

cc @ywcui1990

issue_title: Refactor smudged lines (####, +=+=, ----, etc)
issue_body:
Current code base has a lot of smudged lines used to separate blocks, to present obvious information, or just to decorate class and funcions, which makes some files has inconsistency format with other files:

Examples:

```
###############################################
def myFunction():
```


```
##################
#  MyClass class #
##################

class MyClass:
```

We could remove these smudged lines in order to code base follow PEP convention and by consequence the code base become cleaner.

issue_title: Fixup assert usage in SpatialPooler
issue_body: `assert` is used in SpatialPooler constructor to validate user input.  `assert`'s are ignored when python interpreter is invoked with `-O` argument.  Meanwhile, it's generally bad practice to use `assert` for user input.  Instead, `assert` should be used to validate internal contracts to indicate that the code did something unexpected, independent of user input.

issue_title: Obtain list of shared records on collection
issue_body:
On protected resources, where permissions are enabled, it should be possible for user to list the records on which they have a permission.

# Use cases:

* Bob wants to share some records publicly on his collection. He adds ``system.Everyone`` to the ``read`` permission on some records. Anonymous users can now obtain a list of Bob public records.
* Alice creates records on Bob's collection. She becomes the owner of those records. She must able to list them and delete them.
* Bob creates some records in his collection, on behalf of Alice. He sets the ``read`` permission to Alice on those records. Alice can now see the records that Bob created for her.

# Challenges:

* Couple storage (filtering) and permission backends in an elegant way
* Do not reject guests

# Several strategies:

**1 Query permission backend to obtain list of ids**

Pros:
* easiest to implement
Cons:
* does not scale (``WHERE id IN <millionrecords>``)

**2 Add a permission field in the record data**
Pros:
* seemly easy to implement
Cons:
* must create DB indices on JSON attributes that contain list values (e.g. ``data->permission->read``)

**3 Add permissions notion in storage**
Add new columns on ``records`` table, index and query those columns.
Pros:
* efficient (i.e. scales)
Cons:
* transactions management between storage and permission ?

**4 Move permissions to storage**
Leave in permissions the user principals operations.
Manage permissions from storage. Each record has a set of permissions. 

Pros:
* Clean separation of concern
* Efficient (scales)
Cons:
* a lot of code to rewrite

issue_title: global: SQLite transactions are not properly created
issue_body:
**Scenario:**
- use SQLite
- run something like this:
```python
class Demo(db.Model):
    __tablename__ = 'demo'
    pk = db.Column(sa.Integer, primary_key=True)

... create the application and the tables ...

with app.app_context():
    with db.session.begin_nested():
        d1 = Demo()
        d1.pk = 1
        db.session.add(d1)
    db.session.rollback()
with app.app_context():
    res = Demo.query.all()
    print res
```
You will see that the result is read even though the transaction has been rollbacked.

**Reason:**
We prevented SQLAlchemy from handling transaction scope automatically by changing the `isolation_level` as described here http://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/latest/dialects/sqlite.html#serializable-isolation-savepoints-transactional-ddl
However we did not send our own `BEGIN` request.
Because of this, the savepoints are really committed.

issue_title: global: constraint naming generates too long names for mysql/pg
issue_labels: [{'id': 414887002, 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/inveniosoftware/invenio-db/labels/Lane:%20fast', 'name': 'Lane: fast', 'color': 'ffa868', 'default': False}, {'id': 414887028, 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/inveniosoftware/invenio-db/labels/Need:%20information', 'name': 'Need: information', 'color': '719caa', 'default': False}, {'id': 414887019, 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/inveniosoftware/invenio-db/labels/Size:%20hard', 'name': 'Size: hard', 'color': 'a828a8', 'default': False}, {'id': 269902745, 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/inveniosoftware/invenio-db/labels/Type:%20bug', 'name': 'Type: bug', 'color': 'ff2a00', 'default': False}]
issue_body:
The constraint naming conventions introduced in #65 seems to be generating names without taking MySQL/PostgreSQL limitations into account.

It also seems like that it's not possible to override the naming convention in order to set a name within the limits. The causes problems for e.g. oauthclient/oauth2server and other modules where constraint names generated are too long.

 I've tried to produce a minimal [breaking PR](https://github.com/inveniosoftware/invenio-db/pull/68/commits/444f8be200a217b5057d65ef822ccd454b100b0e) that demonstrates the problem.

Best would be if it was possible to just override the name for a given constraint if the name generated is too long. If that's not possible we may need to remove the naming conventions again, and force that models explicitly specify a constraint name.

issue_body:
Add the ability to publish SSE events, which correspond to a particular deposit.

One possible solution is to register an endpoint to each deposit (i.e. `/deposits/<depid>/sse`), where a GET request would return an established connection with an SSE channel (having the same name as the deposit URL). Therefore, all handling of access rights concerning the SSE subscriptions will be provided by the `read_permission_factory` on the deposit.

issue_title: Drop support for Django < 1.11
issue_body:
With this baitclick title I'd like to start a discussion on when we should drop the support for Django version less than 1.11. My first and most realistic proposition is to wait until 1.8 extended supported is dropped to ensure that everyone has time to update, however maybe we should speed it up slightly e.g. few months before 1.8 death? Everyone that cares about having latest and greatest versions will probably want to upgrade Django to 1.11.x LTS as well anyway. 

Obviously, even when we drop support for particular Django versions everyone will still be able to use versions of djoser compatible with these versions - it's just not recommended to do so, because one does not receive any fixes that way.

As to why it would be convenient - supporting multiple Django versions makes development slower, we could drop few compatibility tweaks, simplify tests, speed up CI etc.

issue_title: Google historical prices not working for Canadian (TSE) stocks
issue_body:
import pandas_datareader.data as web

# This request for historical price data from Google Finance for a US stock works just fine:
result = web.DataReader('AAPL', 'google', start='1970-01-01')

# But for Canadian (TSE) stocks, it fails with: pandas_datareader._utils.RemoteDataError: Unable to read URL: http://www.google.com/finance/historical
result = web.DataReader('TSX:RY', 'google', start='1970-01-01')

# The problem is that pandas_datareader calls Google Finance with the parameter "output=csv". The parameter output=csv is
# however not supported for all stock exchanges, apparently, as also pointed out in this stackoverflow post:
# "However, this format=csv option does not work for a few stock exchanges." (http://stackoverflow.com/questions/11516633/how-to-work-with-google-finance)

# Potential solution: The data is available, just not in CSV format, as can be seen here:
# https://www.google.com/finance/historical?q=tse:azr&startdate=Sep%2016%2C%201970&start=0&num=9999
# Parsing the page is certainly possible, even though I haven't quite found out yet how you can show more than 200
# dates on one page. Currently it looks like you would have to parse several pages, which looks not very attractive.

issue_title: Update readme for 1.3 release
Multiple path parameters and multiple endpoints
issue_body:
Hi there,

I currently have a flask_restful Resource with 2 path parameters: one compulsory, and one optional. I followed [this recommendations](http://stackoverflow.com/a/26379214/6487514) of writing it this way:

`api.add_resource(ProductsResource, '/products/<string:slug>', '/products/<string:slug>/<string:color>')`

` class ExpressProducts(Resource):
      @marshal_with(products_fields)
      def get(self, slug, color=None):`

Then, i have the following YML documentation in the docstring regarding parameters:

`
...
- name: slug
   in: path
   description: product's slug
   type: string
   required: true
- name: color
   in: path
   description: product's color
   type: string
   required: true
`

When I go to my project on http://localhost:5000/spec, I see that I have 2 endpoint generated:

 * http://localhost:5000/products/{slug}
 * http://localhost:5000/products/{slug}/{color}

But, for both endpoints there are those 2 required parameters, resulting in a swagger error: `Path parameter is defined but is not declared: color`. 

Do you think it would be possible to filter the path parameters based on the endpoints used ? Here it would mean filter out 'color' for the first endpoint.

Thanks,
Pierre.

issue_title: Timeout, verify_ssl options not supported by gevent transport
issue_body:
The docs for transport state at the beginning "All transports should support at least the following options" which is then followed by a list of timeout, verify_ssl and ca_certs.

But when trying to use the gevent+http transport, I get an error: `__init__() got an unexpected keyword argument 'timeout'` from `raven.transport.TransportRegistry.get_transport` on the line `self._transports[full_url] = self._schemes[parsed_url.scheme](parsed_url, **options)`

Looking at `raven.transport.gevent.GeventedHTTPTransport` its `__init__` indeed doesn't take these three arguments, but instead just the special `maximum_outstanding_requests` argument.

I think that init method should be changed to also take the three arguments, then pass these through to the super call, which ends up calling the `HTTPTransport.__init__` which indeed does take these arguments.

issue_labels: [{'id': 916959, 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/FortAwesome/Font-Awesome/labels/new%20icon', 'name': 'new icon', 'color': '0e8a16', 'default': False}, {'id': 892571285, 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/FortAwesome/Font-Awesome/labels/released', 'name': 'released', 'color': 'c2e0c6', 'default': False}, {'id': 40518612, 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/FortAwesome/Font-Awesome/labels/web%20application%20icons', 'name': 'web application icons', 'color': 'eb6420', 'default': False}]
issue_body:
I'd love to have a church icon - depicting a church building. This would be very useful for using with the countless church websites that exist.

Something like this would do the trick for me:

![churchicon](https://f.cloud.github.com/assets/1030242/1893005/93831d70-7a65-11e3-9eaa-69444bde5d3b.png)

This one looks really good at all sizes, including when it's small, and it has a nice mix of both black and white, which is similar to many of the other Font Awesome icons.

And I like that the cross doesn't stick up too high above the top of the roof - I've seen some other church icons which have a very tall steeple above the roof, but that makes the church building itself, below the steeple, comparatively smaller which isn't so good. The one I've depicted above allows the church building to take up the majority of the height of the image, with just a small bit of the height taken up by the cross on top. So it's closer in size to the existing "fa-home" icon, in terms of the height of the two buildings.

I would, however, prefer the cross to have square edges rather than rounded edges - I think that would look better. I'd also suggest giving the upper window square edges too, to make its edges more defined especially in smaller sizes.

---

**UPDATE:**

@zuck suggested an alternate style based on the existing Home icon, which I really like. You can follow our discussion in the thread below.

Therefore I'd like to request something like this icon below, rather than my previous suggestion above. I think this one fits much better with the overall style of Font Awesome:

![churches-new](https://cloud.githubusercontent.com/assets/1030242/12766317/8bd83d9a-ca03-11e5-9558-736ae7005f5b.png)

issue_title: Allow more formatting styles
issue_body:
As of now, we allow `human` and `json` for output styles specified by the `--format` option. The human option is a brief snippet of info in a table, while the json is everything that the server serializes.

It would be nice to have some in-between options. I would suggest 2 enhancement features:

 - ability to dump the output as YAML with the nice-looking flow style (easy)
 - ability to specify the fields to be printed with a list-valued option

issue_title: Password visibility
issue_body:
Since #79, no filtering of the connection parameters is done, therefore the password can be visible, for example during pg_basebackup:

    /usr/lib/postgresql/9.5/bin/pg_basebackup --pgdata=/home/postgres/pgdata/data --xlog-method=stream \
    --dbname=postgres://standby:PASSWORDHERE@172.31.157.130:5432/postgres

issue_title: Add patronictl reload function
issue_body:
`patronictl` doesn't have an option to reload the config from the YAML file. Currently you either have to send a SIGHUP to the patroni process or use the REST API to send `POST /reload`.

We should add a `patronictl` option to call the reload function via the API, similar to the restart function.

I'm working on a fork now for this.

Return JSON from timedelta objects

Python 3.5.2

issue_title: Cross-origin resource sharing support
issue_body:
Is it planned to build CORS into connexion? The only way I found was to use the app (flask) attribute of the app (connexion) and [flask-cors](https://flask-cors.readthedocs.org/en/latest/):

```python
import connexion
from flask.ext.cors import CORS

app = connexion.App(__name__, port=8080, specification_dir='swagger/')
app.add_api('test.yaml')

# add CORS support
CORS(app.app)

app.run()
```

issue_title: difficult to know what changed
issue_body:
gengo-python 0.1.26 is the latest release on pypi, but isn't tagged in this repository. Further, there don't appear to be any tags in this repository between 0.1.19 and 0.1.27.

gengo-python 0.1.27 is tagged in this repository (15 hours ago as of this writing), but isn't on pypi.

The `CHANGELOG.rst` is curious and possibly suggests it doesn't get updated very often.

The way this project is being maintained makes it difficult to understand to use. It's hard to know what changed between versions so that I can update my gengo-python-using code appropriately.

Can you backfill the tags and update the CHANGELOG with the releases that are missing? For versions that were skipped and never released, it helps to have a note in the CHANGELOG about that.

issue_title: Améliorer les changelog
issue_body:
J'ouvre cette issue suite à une discussion avec un utilisateur d'openfisca qui effectue des simulations de prestations sociales type mes-aides, via l'api, sans aujourd'hui contribuer.

En discutant avec lui, je me rends compte que le CHANGELOG de `openfisca-france` n'est compréhensible que par des contributeurs qui ont déjà une certaine vue d’ensemble de ce qu’il y a sur openfisca.

Pour les intéressé.e.s, voyons-nous une fois que @MattiSG  sera revenu, pour trouver des pistes d’améliorations.

Quelques idées que j’ai eu après la discussion :

- Ecrire le changelog en français, puisque c’est la langue de travail sur `openfisca-france`
- Taguer les changements, par exemple Aides-sociales, Impots, Cotisations
- Indiquer si les changements impactent la legislation d’aujourd’hui, ou l'historique

issue_title: Fix requests for invalid certificates
issue_body:
Hi!

We are having some problems with this library in OnPremise CARTO instances with invalid certificates and without the chance of use `http`, only `https`.

For fixing this, the [`APIKeyAuthClient`](https://github.com/CartoDB/carto-python/blob/1.0.1/carto/auth.py#L29) class should have a `verify` (or something like that) attribute that refers to the `verify` parameter in [`requests`](http://docs.python-requests.org/en/master/user/advanced/#ssl-cert-verification). We'll need to propagate this change to [`BasicAuthClient`](https://github.com/danicarrion/pyrestcli/blob/v0.6.3/pyrestcli/auth.py#L12), also for setting the using of the `verify` parameter in the `send` method.

We can do that couple of PRs, but we need to now why you convert every boolean to a string [here](https://github.com/danicarrion/pyrestcli/blob/v0.6.3/pyrestcli/auth.py#L74), because the `verify` parameter needs to be a boolean.

Regards.

As per the API documentation - https://docs.atlassian.com/jira/REST/latest/#d2e393, the `/rest/api/2/issue/{issueIdOrKey}/watchers` endpoint accepts `application/json` but example input is "fred" (which seems wrong to me, but that's a different issue!)

From the code, `jira.resources.Watchers` doesn't have an "add" method. However, it inherits `update` method from it's superclass `Resource` so I tried the following and it fails.

```
issue.fields.watches.update(username='vineet')
```

issue_title: Create multiple AWS APIs
issue_body:
Is it possible to create multiple APIs?  For instance, my first API has about 20 endpoints that are specifically created to be used by an ios/android app or javascript browser page.  Now I need another API with a different app_name.  I have many more APIs planned for creation as time goes on and I want them to be separate APIs so I don't end up with a single API containing 100 or more endpoints.
To try to accomplish this I created another chalice folder, but when I did "chalice deploy" it deleted the first API from AWS API Gateway and added the new one.  I need to have the ability to create multiple APIs in Gateway.  Is this possible with chalice and how do I accomplish it?
Thanks

issue_title: Chalice generate-pipeline can create yaml incompatible with Cloudformation
issue_body:
Hi folks,

I was working on a chalice app recently and decided to try out the chalice generate-pipeline feature. My app looked something like this:

```
from chalice import Chalice

app = Chalice(app_name='docGen')

@app.route('/some-path', methods=['POST'])
def generate_stuff():
    json_body = app.current_request.json_body
    return do_the_thing(json_body)
```
I've left out some details, obviously, but this is enough to see the problem. When I ran generate pipeline and deployed that pipeline, it built out a CloudFormation yaml that included this:
```
Resources:
  APIHandler:
    Properties:
      CodeUri: s3://some-s3-bucket
      Events:
        generate_stuffpostc666:
          Properties:
            Method: post
            Path: /some-path
            RestApiId:
              Ref: RestAPI
          Type: Api
      Handler: app.app
      MemorySize: 128
      Role: some-arn
      Runtime: python2.7
      Tags:
        aws-chalice: version=0.10.1:stage=dev:app=docGen
      Timeout: 60
    Type: AWS::Serverless::Function
```
Cool, right? Nope. Cloudformation barfed on this saying something to the effect "Event names must be alphanumeric" I don't have the exact error message anymore cause I went to retrieve it today and the ChangeSet is gone. Anyway, I created a test to reproduce the problem, but I'm not sure how you folks would want to rectify the problem. You can see it here: https://github.com/aws/chalice/compare/master...corespace:view_name?expand=1

issue_title: Help me to correct chalice staging url configuration
pyz3?
issue_body:
Why not use python z3? The wrapper does basically the same things that z3 command line does, and anything that doesn't directly have a function in the library is exposed through other means.

This would be helpful because you could then pip install z3-solver as well, which would remove another package being installed directly to the system.

```
clang: error: linker command failed with exit code 1 (use -v to see invocation)
```

The test is:
```c
#include "dynet/training.h"
#include "dynet/expr.h"
#include "dynet/io.h"
#include "dynet/model.h"

using namespace dynet;

int main(int argc, char const *argv[])
{
    
    ParameterCollection pc;
    SimpleSGDTrainer trainer(pc);
    
    //Build graph
    ComputationGraph cg;

    Expression W = parameter(cg, pc.add_parameters({1, 3}));

    std::vector<real> x_values(3);
    Expression x = input(cg, {3}, &x_values);

    real y_value;
    Expression y = input(cg, &y_value);

    Expression y_pred = logistic(W*x);
    Expression l = binary_log_loss(y_pred, y);

    cg.print_graphviz();

    x_values = {0.5, 0.3, 0.7};
    y_value = 1.0;

    real loss = as_scalar(cg.forward(l));
    trainer.update();
    
    return 0;
}
```
The compiling command is as follows:
```bash
clang test.cc -o test -I/Users/albain/Developer/ML/dynet -L/Users/albain/Developer/ML/dynet/build/dynet -ldynet -std=c++11
```

issue_title: Make matchers composable more easily
issue_body:
We love testtools matchers. We typically build some pretty complex matchers, which make our tests much easier to read. However, we frequently run into issues with the fact that re-using one matcher to implement a second one is tricky. At best it involves writing something like this inside the `match` function:

```python

def match(self, matchee):
    mismatch = SomeOtherMatcher('foo').match(matchee)
    if mismatch:
        return mismatch

    mismatch = YetAnotherMatcher(123).match(matchee)
    if mismatch:
        return mismatch

   # etc.
```


...this gets tiresome quickly. 

Another thing that sometimes happens is engineers try and work around this with subclassing one matcher from another:


```python

class MyMatcher(AnotherMatcher):

    def match(self, matchee):
        mismatch = super().match(matchee)
        if mismatch: 
            return mismatch
        # check more things here.
```

...however this leads to all the classic issues surrounding using inheritance to re-use behavior.


I propose instead that the base `Matcher` class should contain an `assertThat` method, so the first example above could be more natually expressed as:

```python

def match(self, matchee):
    self.assertThat(matchee, SomeOtherMatcher('foo'))
    self.assertThat(matchee, YetAnotherMatcher(123))
   # etc.
```

I'm filing this bug in order to solicit ideas/feedback - I can probably find some time to implement this myself in the next few weeks.

issue_title: Recurrence.between() doesn't exclude Feb 29th when Recurrence has empty dtstart and inclusion is Daily
issue_body:
The following failing test describes the issue:

https://github.com/django-recurrence/django-recurrence/compare/master...alextreme:feb29-exclusion?expand=1

It's an edge-case that only occurs with between() when the Recurrence is daily and the exclusion is Feb 29th and dtstart isn't filled in.

I've been able to easily work around it by either setting dtstart or providing an alternative exclusion rule, but I'm noting this issue in case anyone else stumbles upon it (today or over 4 years).

issue_title: Datastore transactions do not work without context manager
issue_body:
[The docs][docs] state that you can use transactions without a context manager, but this is not exactly true because you need to resort to some private methods on `Client`:

* [this file][context] uses the context manager and produces correct output
* [this file][broken] uses transactions as described by the aforementioned docs and fails the assertion
* [this file][working] uses transactions along with calls to `client._push_batch` and `client._pop_batch` and produces correct output

The issue is evident in [`Client.get_multi`][get_multi] and [`Client.put_multi`][put_multi] where `transaction` and `current` are `None` unless a `Batch` or `Transaction` has been pushed to the `_batch_stack` and the only time that happens is when you [`__enter__`][enter] a `Batch`.

[docs]: https://googlecloudplatform.github.io/gcloud-python/stable/datastore-transactions.html#gcloud.datastore.transaction.Transaction
[context]: https://gist.github.com/Bogdanp/a86192d1a2a37dd48df5ebdbb39a49d1#file-context-py-L20
[broken]: https://gist.github.com/Bogdanp/a86192d1a2a37dd48df5ebdbb39a49d1#file-failing-py-L20
[working]: https://gist.github.com/Bogdanp/a86192d1a2a37dd48df5ebdbb39a49d1#file-passing-py-L20
[get_multi]: https://github.com/GoogleCloudPlatform/gcloud-python/blob/master/gcloud/datastore/client.py#L284
[put_multi]: https://github.com/GoogleCloudPlatform/gcloud-python/blob/master/gcloud/datastore/client.py#L336
[enter]: https://github.com/GoogleCloudPlatform/gcloud-python/blob/master/gcloud/datastore/batch.py#L273

issue_title: BurstyPool in spanner can lead to unbounded number of sessions to be created
issue_body:
BurstyPool takes a parameter which is supposed to bound the maximum number of sessions that will be maintained in the pool. But this invariant if maintained when the session is put back in the pool. In case there is a bug (either in the client or in the user code) which leads to session leaking and never being released back into the pool, this can cause an unbounded number of sessions to be created. That can be dangerous since sessions expire only after an hour on the backend so this can cause a user to quickly hit the session limit on the backend and then in effect be locked out of the database for upto an hour.
Instead the behavior should be changed to make the user wait in `get` if the existing number of sessions that have been created is equal to the max size.

issue_title: Ubuntu cmdline arg for console doesn't specify 38400 baud rate (fallbacks to 9600?)
issue_body:
I've analyzed all the kernel command line arguments for ubuntu images:
```
ubuntu 14.04: BOOT_IMAGE=/boot/vmlinuz-4.4.0-124-generic root=UUID=17e286d8-90e7-4e3f-8eda-b920ff2849f0 ro scsi_mod.use_blk_mq=Y console=ttyS0o
ubuntu 16.04: BOOT_IMAGE=/boot/vmlinuz-4.13.0-1015-gcp root=LABEL=cloudimg-rootfs ro scsi_mod.use_blk_mq=Y console=ttyS0
ubuntu 17.10: BOOT_IMAGE=/boot/vmlinuz-4.13.0-41-generic root=LABEL=cloudimg-rootfs ro scsi_mod.use_blk_mq=Y console=ttyS0
ubuntu 18.04: BOOT_IMAGE=/boot/vmlinuz-4.15.0-1006-gcp root=LABEL=cloudimg-rootfs ro scsi_mod.use_blk_mq=Y console=ttyS0
```
The console argument should be `ttyS0,38400n8` (See GoogleCloudPlatform/compute-image-tools#402). According to a [the documentation](https://www.kernel.org/doc/html/v4.15/admin-guide/kernel-parameters.html), the baudrate will be set to 9600 if not explicitly set.

Is that a real concern?

issue_title: Updating or Deleting a property from an entity by name (Java)
global imports
issue_body:
I know that dill can have issues pickling global variables, but I was wondering if the following case is intended. My use case for this is setting up a job on a given machine, pickling the instructions, sending it over to remote machines, unpickling the objects, and running the jobs there.

Run the following in one file.

```
import dill
import numpy as np

def test():
  return np

if __name__ == "__main__":
  with open("out", "w") as f:
    f.write(dill.dumps(test))
```

Run the next in a different file. Running in a different file is necessary to emulate the environment I am using, where the numpy import is not included at the target machine.

```
import dill

with open("out") as f:
  contents = f.read()

res = dill.loads(contents)

print(res())
```

This yields:
NameError: global name 'np' is not defined

However, the code works if, instead of the first file, I define the function ```test``` in a separate file (so that it is in a formal namespace, not "__main__", load and pickle it from a separate runner, the numpy import also gets packaged and unpickled successfully.

Is there some way to get around this? Incidentally, setting ```dill.settings["recurse"] = True``` fixes this particular issue, but in my actual script, doing so raises:

```
RuntimeError: maximum recursion depth exceeded in cmp
```

issue_title: Is SASS-style "@extend" weird?
issue_body:
I'm trying to migrate my projects from Less to Sass and occasionally comparing preprocessor output to make sure I'm not doing anything wrong (totally new to sass world).

I had the initial impression that the `@extend` of Sass and the `:extend` of Less were doing pretty much the same thing. However `@extend` seems to be doing its job more "aggressively".

There are 8 places where `@extend` is used in Bootstrap's Sass port, and one of them is in `_button-groups.scss`:

```scss
.btn-group-lg > .btn { @extend .btn-lg; } // line 98
```
Less side: (`button-groups.less`)

```less
.btn-group-lg > .btn { &:extend(.btn-lg); } // line 98
```

When compiled, Sass inserts (frantically, imho) `.btn-group-lg > .btn` and its variations for every single matching `.btn-lg` selector. For instance, one of the rules produced by Less is:

```css
.btn-group > .btn-lg + .dropdown-toggle {
    padding-right: 12px;
    padding-left: 12px;
}
```
Sass, however creates this:

```css
.btn-group > .btn-lg + .dropdown-toggle,
.btn-group-lg.btn-group > .btn + .dropdown-toggle {
    padding-right: 12px;
    padding-left: 12px;
}
```
All the components that use `@extend` (navs, input-groups, button-groups) similarly produces extra rules. (Rules that are not present in Less output) Is this the normal behavior? Is it because I use node-sass or somehing else?

I've created an example app for the comparison:
https://github.com/monoblaine/bootstrap-3-sass-extend-oddity

issue_title: PyCharm autocompletion for properties
issue_body:
Due to the fact that the classes have dynamically generated fields for the AWS resource properties, IDEs like PyCharm doesn't suggest those fields during autocompletion.

For example:
```Python
from troposphere import Template, apigateway

t = Template()

apiResource = apigateway.Resource("contactcloud")
apiResource.ParentId #<-- won't be suggested by autocompletion!
```

This could be remedied in two ways:

### 1. Manually add type hints to the beginning of classes, e.g.:
```Python
class Resource(AWSObject):
    ParentId: basestring
    PathPart: basestring
    RestApiId: basestring
    
    resource_type = "AWS::ApiGateway::Resource"
    
    props = {
        "ParentId": (basestring, True),
        "PathPart": (basestring, True),
        "RestApiId": (basestring, True)
    }
```

### 2. Automatically generate stub files from classes
This is outlined by [PEP 484#stub-files](https://www.python.org/dev/peps/pep-0484/#stub-files)

issue_title: improve pex caching + resolve behavior
issue_body:
we get many reports of pex resolve failures with errors like:

```
Exception message: Could not satisfy all requirements for greenlet>=0.4.9:
    greenlet>=0.4.9(from: gevent==1.1.1), greenlet>=0.3(from: eventlet==0.19.0)
```

which tend to disappear after cleaning the pex cache and tend to never be reproducible when it comes to filing bug reports. the one data dump I did ever manage to capture on this in the past failed to reproduce locally (which I now believe to be due to mtime matching on the reinstated cache).

while troubleshooting with someone on slack, I noticed this via `./pants help-advanced python-setup`:

```
--python-setup-resolver-cache-ttl=<seconds>
    The time in seconds before we consider re-resolving an open-ended
    requirement, e.g. "flask>=0.2" if a matching distribution is available on
    disk.
```    

which seems to imply that whenever a package is cached, that that version will become sticky for open-ended resolves of the same package - even transitively. as we've seen, this can and will cause issues with cross-project/branch resolves that happen in the same TTL window.

we should look for a way to improve this behavior while still netting the performance gains of the cache itself - or potentially eliminate caching for open-ended resolves.

issue_title: Explode during planning for "missing" configuration
issue_body:
There should be a (ideally declarative) way to explode if necessary configuration is missing for a target.

This will help to support #2484, by allowing us to switch from "ambiguous configuration/promises cause errors" to "missing configuration causes an error".

issue_title: Deal with legacy deploy_excludes intransitive mode support
issue_body: Currently we support a global legacy mode via `JvmBinaryTasks`'s `--shallow-deploy-excludes`.  This support helps out Square for one who relies on the shallow deploy_exclude behavior to cherry-pick jars out of a binary deployjar and then place them elsewhere in a custom bundle with a custom task.  The option doesn't make alot of sense as a global one though in the general case, it would make more sense if the intransitive bit could be written down in `JvmBinary` targets.  Either support this level of customization or deprecate and remove the global option once users have an alternate path forward.

issue_title: Bundle resource jars do not include resource parent dirs.
issue_body:
For example, applying:
```diff
diff --git a/testprojects/src/java/org/pantsbuild/testproject/ideacodeandresources/BUILD b/testprojects/src/java/org/pantsbuild/testproject/ideacodeandresources/BUILD
index 1c7aba8..0f33a6f 100644
--- a/testprojects/src/java/org/pantsbuild/testproject/ideacodeandresources/BUILD
+++ b/testprojects/src/java/org/pantsbuild/testproject/ideacodeandresources/BUILD
@@ -16,3 +16,10 @@ resources(
   sources=['README.md'],
 )
 
+jvm_binary(
+  name='bin',
+  main='None!',
+  dependencies=[
+    ':code',
+  ],
+)
```

Then running `./pants clean-all bundle testprojects/src/java/org/pantsbuild/testproject/ideacodeandresources:bin`, yields:
```
$ zipinfo -1 dist/testprojects.src.java.org.pantsbuild.testproject.ideacodeandresources.bin-bundle/libs/testprojects.src.java.org.pantsbuild.testproject.ideacodeandresources.readme-0.jar 
org/pantsbuild/testproject/ideacodeandresources/README.md
```

Java jar files generally need all parent dir entries for any given entry to enable expected use cases and we enforce this with [tests](https://github.com/pantsbuild/pants/blob/master/tests/java/org/pantsbuild/tools/jar/JarBuilderTest.java#L411-421) for our java [JarBuilder](https://github.com/pantsbuild/pants/blob/master/src/java/org/pantsbuild/tools/jar/JarBuilder.java) code.

issue_title: python_binary_create task does not respect default platforms from python-setup
issue_body:
The python_binary_create task does not take default platform arguments from python-setup into account when creating a pex file; only the platforms from the binary target in question are plumbed through to pex builder.

This task needs to prefer target-level constraints, and if those are empty, use the config provided by the python-setup object.

issue_title: Make it easy to mix options and tools into cache keys
issue_body:
We used to have invalidate_for() and invalidate_for_files() [the latter still exists but is dead code.]

It would be nice to have something like that again, so that tasks that simply want to qualify cache keys with, say, a setting or a tool version, don't have to explicitly mess with FingerprintStrategy.

issue_title: Design new PEX entrypoints to expose PEXBuilder
issue_body:
This is a tracking ticket for:
* https://github.com/pantsbuild/pex/issues/490
* https://github.com/pantsbuild/pex/issues/491

----

A core portion of the work accomplished by the `gather_sources.py` and `resolve_requirements_task_base.py` files is collecting their inputs into PEXBuilders.

Currently we build two(ish) pexes: one for the sources, and one for the requirements... and in both cases this is accomplished by usage of the `PEXBuilder.add_*` methods (for `sources`, `resources`, `requirements`).

In order to create mergeable [0] pexes from sources and requirements using `@rule`s, we should expose the necessary CLI APIs from PEX.
