issue_title: Replace the os_is_* functions
issue_body:
The `os_is_*` functions set a bad precedent. There's an enormous number of combinations of system facts that one might want to discover, and having `os_is_*` functions is a recipe for an explosion in number of functions. A framework should provide more generalized tools that individual users can adapt to their needs.

A good solution would be to provide `get_os_release` or `get_redhat_release` functions that would return a dict of key/value pairs. Plugins could then write code like:

```python
def os_is_f27():
    os_release = get_os_release()
    return os_release['ID'] == 'fedora' and os_release['VERSION_ID'] == '27'
```

Or, if the implementation of that becomes problematic, something like this would still be much nicer:

```python
def os_is_f27():
    return get_os_release_id() == 'fedora' and get_os_release_version_id == '27'
```

issue_title: Order of Middlewares
issue_body:
I've discovered that this line inverts the middleware order:
https://github.com/graphql-python/graphql-core/blob/6df8a6312b579a6a1454bcf29a566ce5d0fa9849/graphql/execution/middleware.py#L51

This was somewhat confusing, as I expected the first installed middleware to be the entrypoint. I.e. 

```python
schema.execute(
    statement,
    middleware=[mw1, mw2],
)
```

actually produces the call stack `mw2 -> mw1 -> resolve_func`, whereas i'd expected `mw1 -> mw2 -> resolve_func`.

I'm not clear if this is a bug, as this functionality isn't tested anywhere, or if it would just go in documentation somewhere.

issue_title: Support 'path' in error reporting
Support 'path' in error reporting
issue_body:
When dealing with error responses on client side, would be nice to know which field caused it. Right now there seems to be support for `locations` -> `(column: .., line: ...)`, but not for the `path` specified in https://github.com/facebook/graphql/pull/230/

> If an error can be associated to a particular field in the GraphQL result, it must contain an entry with the key path that details the path of the response field which experienced the error. This allows clients to identify whether a null result is intentional or caused by a runtime error.
> 
> This field should be a list of path segments starting at the root of the response and ending with the field associated with the error. Path segments that represent fields should be strings, and path segments that represent list indices should be 0‐indexed integers. If the error happens in an aliased field, the path to the error should use the aliased name, since it represents a path in the response, not in the query.

```
  "errors": [
    {
      "message": "Name for character with ID 1002 could not be fetched.",
      "locations": [ { "line": 6, "column": 7 } ],
      "path": [ "hero", "heroFriends", 1, "name" ]
    }
  ],
```

Note: might take a look at this at some point myself (would like to also fine-tune when server side exception prints traceback to logs and/or figuring out how to gracefully report errors to client without exception + traceback on server...)

issue_title: "Ignore outliers in chart scaling" made me think my early training data was lost
issue_body:
I started retraining a model from scratch after changing its implementation, and I wanted to compare my results from the first few epochs between the two versions of the model. I went on Tensorboard for the original model and realized I couldn't see the accuracy for the first 3-4 epochs at all.

I ended up believing that the first few epochs had been lost somehow until @reedwm suggested unchecking "Ignore outliers in chart scaling." Once I did, I magically got all my data back.

I understand the rationale for having this feature is to zoom in on the more recent data which the user is probably more interested in, rather than having to zoom out to show the initial steep climb/drop. I wouldn't mind if it were simply a default zoom option, where you could still zoom out to see the rest of your data. However, if you aren't aware of this checkbox, your "outlier" data is completely inaccessible.

cc @chihuahua

issue_title: Converting 2.5 criteria files to 3.0 criteria files
issue_body:
It came up at the workshop that it would be handy if the PmagPy 2.5 --> 3.0 conversion could handle criteria files, so I took a stab at it.

I didn’t have all the data I need in acceptance_criteria to write a valid 3.0 criteria file.  

    {'samples.int_n_specimens': {'category': None,
      'decimal_points': -999,
      'threshold_type': 'inherited',
      'value': '3'},
     ….


So I modified pmag.read_criteria_from_file to include some additional data (‘pmag_criteria_code’, ‘er_citation_names’, and ‘criteria_definition’ if available):

    {'sample_int_n': {'category': None,
      'criteria_definition': 'acceptance criteria for study',
      'decimal_points': -999,
      'er_citation_names': 'This study',
      'pmag_criteria_code': 'ACCEPT',
      'threshold_type': 'inherited',
      'value': '3'},


Then I wrote a new function, pmag.convert_criteria_file_2_to_3, which: 
- uses the data from pmag.read_criteria_from_file
- get criterion_operation from the data model
- and assembles everything into proper 3.0 format.

Everything looked good, but then I realized that I was missing a few items.  My conversion script works properly if there is only one criterion (ACCEPT, IE-SPEC, etc), but can break with multiple criteria.

The problem is that pmag.read_criteria_from_file stores values in a dictionary, and so if a table-column is used in more than one place, the value will be overwritten.  

i.e., if a criteria file looks like this (McMurdo 2.5):

    criteria_definition	er_citation_names	pmag_criteria_code    site_polarity

    Criteria for inclusion in normal mean	This study	NPOLE	    n  
    Criteria for inclusion in reverse mean	This study	RPOLE	    r

pmag.read_criteria_from_file will munch it and end up with only one of the two rows needed for site_polarity, since you can't have a dictionary with two 'site_polarity' keys. 

I could write a second version of pmag.read_criteria_from_file that stores those data differently, but that seems a bit redundant.  I could also re-write pmag.read_criteria_from_file to store the data a little differently {"site_polarity": {}, "site_polarity2": {}}, or something like that, and then rename them at the very end.  I'm just a bit nervous about breaking criteria functionality elsewhere.  

@ltauxe, I know you are out and about but maybe we can discuss this when you get back?

issue_title: Line breaks and training
issue_body:
I think it would be good for an option of allowing newlines to prevent across-lines training.

I created word vectors using a dataset where each entry was per line. Across-line correlations would be noise. The loss was low, but could be lower if it wasn't trying to predict across lines.

(I assume that newlines don't affect training... although I'd be happy to be told otherwise.)

issue_title: Suggested way to read/stream the word representations in python
issue_body:
I'm wondering what is the suggested way to read/stream the word representations? It seems extremely show naively implemented.

It seems very weird to end up with a matrix like (ignore the values of the numbers):

```python
I 0.3 0.3 0.3
have 0.3 0.3 0.3
fun 0.3 0.3 0.3
I 0.3 0.3 0.3
am 0.3 0.3 0.3
happy 0.3 0.3 0.3
```

2 observations:
- I could not find a sentence boundary, so it is not easy to read back cases/lines.
- In case you really just use it as a word representation, then it does not make sense to have the words occur multiple times.

I'm curious to hear what the logic behind this format is, and hopefully, a nice way to read it fast in python :)

issue_title: DataFrameMapper cannot leverage special fit_transform methods
issue_body:
For transformers in a DataFrameMapper, it would be useful to be able to access native combined fit_transform methods. This would allow e.g. for the different semantics of PCA(..., copy=False).fit_transform(X) versus .fit(X).transform(X), or the optimized version of DictVectorizer.fit_transform for sparse matrices, etc.

The current implementation of DataFrameMapper can only access each transformers fit and transform method successively. It's implementaion of fit_transform is the default inherited from TransformerMixin which always calls fit(...).transform(...).  

It should be quite simple to extend DataFrameMapper to account for this issue. I have the code prepared and will create a PR.

issue_title: Add automatic Windows driver configuration
How to use Basescript `init_logger` in `django`.
issue_body:
Useful for getting structured logs.



| Without basescript| 
| -------- | 
| ![](https://i.imgur.com/nsS0swI.png)|

| With basescript|
|--------------- |
| **stdout** | 
| ![](https://i.imgur.com/ZZ3TJix.png)|
| **log file (web.log)** | 
| ![](https://i.imgur.com/gGOf2Xc.png)
|

### Install basescript
```
pip3 install basescript
```

#### Modifications in `settings.py`
```
from basescript import init_logger

MIDDLEWARE = [
    'django.middleware.security.SecurityMiddleware',
    'django.contrib.sessions.middleware.SessionMiddleware',
    'django.middleware.common.CommonMiddleware',
    'django.middleware.csrf.CsrfViewMiddleware',
    'django.contrib.auth.middleware.AuthenticationMiddleware',
    'django.contrib.messages.middleware.MessageMiddleware',
    'django.middleware.clickjacking.XFrameOptionsMiddleware',
    
    'deepsieve_api.middleware.LoggerMiddleware', #add the created middleware
]
LOGGING_CONFIG = None

init_logger(
	fmt = None,
	quiet = False,
        level = 'INFO',
        fpath = '/var/logs/web.log',
        pre_hooks = [],
        post_hooks = [],
        metric_grouping_interval = 1)
```

#### Arbitary `views.py`
```
from basescript import get_logger

log = get_logger()

 def signup(self, req: Request, username: str, email: str, password: str) -> str:
         
        #Add custom logs here 
        req.raw_request.log.info('sdsd')
        
        if not all([username, email, password]):
            raise Exception('Need all of username, email and password')
        user = User.objects.create_user(
            username=username, email=email, password=password)
        return user.id
```

#### Configuring middleware (optional)

##### Create a `middleware.py` : To override the default logger of django with custom logs using basescript

Example:
```
from basescript import get_logger

log = get_logger()

class LoggerMiddleware(object):
    def __init__(self, get_response):
        self.get_response = get_response

    def __call__(self, request):
        user = request.user
        _log = log.bind(is_authenticated=bool(user.is_authenticated))
        if user.is_authenticated:
            _log = _log.bind(user=dict(username=user.username, id=user.id))
        request.log = _log
        return self.get_response(request)
```

issue_title: Support for connecting multiple cameras
issue_body:
It would be nice if Harvester would support connecting multiple cameras. This would be useful especially in the field of spectral imaging, where multiple cameras could be controlled with the same interface (Harvester).

I would imagine that this wouldn't be too difficult to implement. Connecting the devices would happen in a usual way with the `connect_device` function, but a new process would be created for the each camera to be connected. If one calls the `connect_device` function with a camera that is already connected, an exception would be raised.

issue_title: google.datalab.utils.Iterator.__iter__ stuck in "infinite" loop
issue_body:
## Cause
The `count` argument of `_get_row_fetcher` should be the running count: https://github.com/googledatalab/pydatalab/blob/ec06b2c1fa4b41be75c7af18c07562afc0a609c3/google/datalab/bigquery/_table.py#L533-L536

But `google.datalab.utils.Iterator.__iter__` is passing in a constant count instead: https://github.com/googledatalab/pydatalab/blob/eec5048c16f1ea0d7be9c2ac5ec49297be6995ae/google/datalab/utils/_iterator.py#L36

## Fix
Change https://github.com/googledatalab/pydatalab/blob/eec5048c16f1ea0d7be9c2ac5ec49297be6995ae/google/datalab/utils/_iterator.py#L40-L41 to `self._count += len(items)`

## How to reproduce the error
The following code is *supposed* to retrieve only 1025 rows. Instead, it first retrieves 1024 rows an HTTP call, then 1 row per HTTP call until the end of the table.

```python
import google.datalab.bigquery as bq
table = bq.Table('some table with > 2000 rows')

for i, row in enumerate(table.range(start_row=1, max_rows=1025)):
    print(i, row)
```

issue_title: latest version 0.9.0 does not allow compression of empty string
issue_body:
Previous versions of python-lz4 allowed compression of the empty string:

```
In [1]: lz4.compress('')
Out[1]: b'\x00\x00\x00\x00\x01\x00\x00\x00\x01\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00\x88/\xc9\x0b'
```

the latest version fails:

```
In [2]: lz4.compress('')
lz4/__init__.py:18: DeprecationWarning: Call to deprecated function or method compress (use lz4.block.compress instead).
  def compress(*args, **kwargs):
---------------------------------------------------------------------------
ValueError                                Traceback (most recent call last) in <module>()
----> 1 lz4.compress('')

lz4/deprecated.py in new_func(*args, **kwargs)
     37             warnings.warn_explicit(msg, category=DeprecationWarning, filename=filename, lineno=lineno)
     38             warnings.simplefilter('default', DeprecationWarning)  # reset filter
---> 39             return cls_or_func(*args, **kwargs)
     40 
     41         return new_func

lz4/__init__.py in compress(*args, **kwargs)
     17 @deprecated('use lz4.block.compress instead')
     18 def compress(*args, **kwargs):
---> 19     return lz4.block.compress(*args, **kwargs)
     20 
     21 @deprecated('use lz4.block.decompress instead')

ValueError: Input source data size invalid: 0 bytes
```


Was there a reason for this change? We use lz4 for compression of lots of data we write to a database. Some of the records might contain empty strings. If I were to upgrade to the latest version of lz4 I'd no longer be able to support that, and I'd also no longer be able to decompress the compressed data that's already been written.

issue_title: Create documentation
issue_body:
This issue encompasses all the documentation requirements of Raiden.

The documentation will be written in [Markdown | Restructured Text] format and imported into a pleasantly looking and easily readable website for our users to browse using [readthedocs](https://docs.readthedocs.io/en/latest/getting_started.html). The doc files should all be located under the `docs/` directory.

The documentation has three sub-targets:

- [ ] **System Overview and User Guide**

    A generic protocol overview, an explanation of how the system work and what all its basic functions are. Also installation and usage guide could be part of this.

- [ ]  **Raiden API**

    Detailed documentation of each API function, usage, calling constraints and requirements

- [ ] **Raiden Specification**
   
    As discussed, since Raiden implementation is changing constantly this may be the hardest to document properly and keep up to date. Let's do our best. A good compromise until things solidify would be to create some sort of a spec documentation by parsing the docstrings directly from the code. If such a tool exists we should use it.

issue_title: Smoketest requires solc even if precompiled ABIs are present
issue_body:
## Problem Definition

Currently the `smoketest` subcommand unconditionally requires `solc` to be installed even if it might be unnecessary (e.g. in binary distributions that ship with precompiled ABIs)

## Solution

Only require that *either* `solc` or the precompiled ABIs are present (possibly allow forcing one of the two behaviors via cli parameter)

## Tasklist

- [ ] Check that either `solc` or all precompiled ABIs are present
- [ ] (Optional) Add cli parameter to force either `solc` or precompiled check
- [ ] Add a changelog entry.

issue_title: Route discovery will not use the best possible path
issue_body:
## Problem Definition

Route discovery algorithm picks first of the neighbour nodes, sorted by these criteria:

1. node status
2. number of hops to the target
3. funds in the channel

Node status is determined by the healthcheck() greenlet, which periodically tries to send Ping message to the other node. If node replies with an Ack, channel status is set to 'reachable', otherwise 'unknown' is set. Node status is set 'unknown' by default, even without the check running.

However, the greenlet is not started by default for each neighbor, but only for targets of a transfer. Because of this, transfers are routed in the network more or less randomly, depending on targets of previous transfers.

## Solution

Either change the routing algorithm sorting criteria to not prefer node by its status, or start healthcheck greenlet for each neighbor of the node.

## Tasklist

- [x] Discuss which solution to choose
- [x] Change the route planner/healthcheck startup

issue_title: Check if ROI type is correctly set when reading from DICOM
issue_body:
We have currently (https://github.com/pytrip/pytrip/blob/v1.2.0/pytrip/vdx.py#L741)

```
    def get_roi_type_number(self, type_name):
        """
        :returns: 1 if GTV or CTV, else 0.
        """
        if type_name == 'EXTERNAL':
            return 0  # TODO: should be 10?
        elif type_name == 'AVOIDANCE':
            return 0
        elif type_name == 'ORGAN':
            return 0
        elif type_name == 'GTV':
            return 1
        elif type_name == 'CTV':
            return 1
        else:
            return 0
```

on the other hand, in TRiP98 documentation (http://bio.gsi.de/DOCS/TRiP98/DOCS/dcm2trip.README.TXT) we find:

```
The vdx files must be edited manually with a text editor. Lines beginning
with the keyword "voi" indicate VOIs. dcm2trip assigns "type 1" to all VOIs.
Instead VOI types should be:

1 for the target
2 for the OARs
10 for the external contour
```

issue_title: Executing input groups concurrently by default
issue_body: Same logic for `concurrent=True` for tasks, it is even difficult to set up a case for `concurrent=False` for input groups, so it makes sense to set `concurrent=True` as default.

issue_title: Input parameter of actions.
issue_body:
The input parameter of actions actually serves two purposes now:

1. for script running actions, the content of the input will be prepended to the script.
2. if both `input` and `output` exist, sos will try to create a task level signature to skip the action.

I did not realize this problem when we added feature 1. It is a bad idea to let one parameter server two purposes.... for example, if a user wants to create step signature with some arbitrary `input`, then the script will be broken.

issue_title: Implement a standard date partitioning strategy for indexed data relying on index names
issue_body:
In gitlab by @sfalquier on Dec 29, 2017, 11:20

Relying on #138 , implement a date partitioning system such as index-root-name-YYYY-MM :
- `index-root-name` will be defined in collection reference (indexPatternPath) and cannot contains wildcards or multi-indeces definitions (such as coma separated indeces)
- `YYYY-MM` will be defined as default date pattern
- a custom date pattern could be set in collection's custom params and must be consistant with ISO 8601 date patterns (https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ISO_8601)
- date partitioning system should be an optional feature when creating a collection reference and collections that use this strategy must be flagged easily (simply by checking their custom params perhaps?)

As a result, for those collections, Explore API response for time filtered requests have to restrict their ES requests to relevant indeces (that is to say use the proper indeces pattern for the ES request, for example `index-root-name-2017-12` or `index-root-name-2017-*`)

python:

issue_title: Pillow 5.1 is broken on OS X
issue_body:
When installing `torch` and `torchvision` in a clean virtualenv with `python3.6` and the corresponding `pip` on `OS X`

```
import torchvision
```

leads to 

```
torch-venv/lib/python3.6/site-packages/PIL/_imaging.cpython-36m-darwin.so, 2): Symbol not found: _clock_gettime
torch-venv/lib/python3.6/site-packages/PIL/.dylibs/liblzma.5.dylib (which was built for Mac OS X 10.12)
  Expected in: /usr/lib/libSystem.B.dylib
```

This is a known bug see [Pillow Issue 3068](https://github.com/python-pillow/Pillow/issues/3068)

As a pytorch user it  can be fixed by specifying the Pillow version in your `requirements.txt`

```
Pillow!=5.1.0
torch
torchvision
```

The `setup.py` should get updated with something like:

```
install_requires=['Pillow != 5.1.0 ; platform_system = "Darwin"']
```

issue_title: get_connected_vnics() returns NICs as URIs
issue_body:
`VirtualSwitch.get_connected_vnics()` returns the NICs that are connected to the virtual switch.
The current design returns these NICs with their URIs. This is inconsistent to other list functions that return resources as their corresponding Python object (`Nic` in this case).

Proposal: Return the connected NICs as a list of `Nic` objects that have just their `object-uri` property set.

Whenever the user first logs in using any oauth2 backend (in my case google). The User gets created in the User table  in django.

I need to set the is_staff to true each time a new user record gets created. How can I do that?

issue_title: Allow disabling Ray parallelism for debugging.
issue_body: This was the intention of `ray.PYTHON_MODE`, but it doesn't work with actors and it needs to be updated.

issue_title: Race condition for `db_connect`
issue_body:
For many Ray components, a common pattern for startup is:
1. Connect to the database
2. Register with a unique client ID in the database
3. Subscribe to relevant channels (e.g. object table notifications)

There is a race condition between steps 2 and 3. If another Ray client receives the client ID registration and publishes to the channel in step 3, the connecting client will not receive the notification. For example, in `local_scheduler.cc:start_server`, we first call `db_connect`, and then we call `task_table_subscribe` to start receiving scheduling notifications from the global scheduler.

Proposed fix: Separate `db_connect` into `db_connect` and `db_register`. `db_connect` initializes the Redis contexts but does not make the client visible to the rest of the clients in the system. `db_register` makes the client visible to the rest of the clients in the system by registering the client ID. This would allow us to do all subscriptions (step 3) before registering the client (step 2).

issue_title: Ray does not support cython?
issue_body:
i wrote a simple cython file which only has one actor with __init__ function, build and setup it.
```
@ray.remote
class SelfPlayer(object):
    def __init__(self):
        os.environ["CUDA_VISIBLE_DEVICES"] = ",".join([str(i) for i in ray.get_gpu_ids()])
```

In another python file, i import that module and try to initialize the SelfPlayer with ray.remote
`rewards = [selfplayers[i].do_selfplay.remote(args) for i in xrange(n_gpu)]`

then i got the following error
```
Traceback (most recent call last):

    train(args)
  File "policy/rlpolicy_train.py", line 57, in train
    selfplayers = [SelfPlayer.remote() for _ in xrange(n_gpu)]

    actor_object._manual_init(*args, **kwargs)
  File "/usr/local/lib/python2.7/dist-packages/ray/actor.py", line 434, in _manual_init
    signature.check_signature_supported(v, warn=True)
  File "/usr/local/lib/python2.7/dist-packages/ray/signature.py", line 47, in check_signature_supported
    in funcsigs.signature(func).parameters.items()]
  File "/usr/local/lib/python2.7/dist-packages/funcsigs/__init__.py", line 62, in signature
    sig = signature(obj.__func__)
  File "/usr/local/lib/python2.7/dist-packages/funcsigs/__init__.py", line 187, in signature
    raise ValueError('callable {0!r} is not supported by signature'.format(obj))

```

issue_title: [DataFrame] Convert PyArrow Table to Ray Dataframe with Zero Copy
issue_body: If I have a variable that is a (large) PyArrow Table - is there anyway to convert this to a Ray DataFrame with zero copy (or minimal copy)?

issue_title: Cleanup the Windows build
issue_body:
The current build tools in `win32/build-with-bootstrap.sh` use an older version of livestreamer and injects a new egg into it. It looks like it might be a mess to fix to our needs, so we might have to rewrite the build process for windows.

I've looked at the options, and it looks like [cx_Freeze](http://cx-freeze.readthedocs.io/en/latest/index.html) could fit our needs, it creates a standalone .exe from a python package and can even [create an installer](http://cx-freeze.readthedocs.io/en/latest/distutils.html#bdist-msi) for it.

issue_title: youtube-dl?
issue_body: Since the project is being forked, this seems like a good opportunity to discuss how this project may relate to `youtube-dl`.  There is some duplicated functionality and purpose between the two projects, and `youtube-dl` is a vibrant project.  Would it make sense to consider porting the functionality exclusive to livestreamer/streamlink to `youtube-dl` instead of carrying this project forward as its own project?

issue_title: Coding style and code policies
issue_body:
I have been bugged by some "stylistically strange" parts on the code. Personally, I like to follow, for the most part, pep8. The line length is a different topic (see below), but without considering that, a call to `pep8 --ignore=E501 ./django-helpdesk` yields 872 lines, of a total of 36 different complains.

Regarding the long lines, a quick look at them gives:
- 1974 lines longer than 79 characters
- 982 lines longer than 99 characters
- 414 lines longer than 119 characters
- 220 lines longer than 139 characters

Long lines are counted with multiplicity.

There are a lot of long lines in `migrations` and also `south_migrations`. Maybe in the branch 0.2 the migrations can be squashed and cleaned up... ok, that is a different topic. But note that without both migration folders, there are 831 non-length issues and 449 long lines (>79).

My question would be, it is a good idea to bring a major clean-up? Do you feel that it is worth to follow pep8 (from now on)? Or you feel that it is better to go beyond that and use `pylint`?

It is a tedious task, but it is quite straightforward. I don't mind to prepare a PR for that, but I am not sure if you agree on that topic, or maybe prefer to follow a subset of the `pep8` may be followed, or go all out with `pylint`.

issue_title: pybel.from_bio returns an empty graph.
issue_body:
Hello everyone,

I am trying to convert [KGML](https://www.kegg.jp/kegg/xml/) into BEL. I have translated KGMLs into the BioPax format (level 3) using the [KEGGtranslator](http://www.cogsys.cs.uni-tuebingen.de/software/KEGGtranslator/) tool. Howerver when I finally load it using pybel I get an empty graph.

`import pybel
graph = pybel.from_indra_statements('hsa04060.translated')

pybel.to_json(graph)           # return empty JSON graph

issue_title: Doc s/we/xxx/; who are "we?"
issue_body:
Documentation uses "we" a lot, esp. in https://trio.readthedocs.io/en/latest/reference-core.html

It's ambiguous, it could be interpreted:
* "we the authors of trio library that you the developer are using," or
* "we the developers who write async functions"

Also, it seems "we" stands for several things:
* "We think this way is easier to reason about." -- sounds like "we who wrote trio"
* "then we propagate it." -- sounds like "trio runtime"
* "and we couldn’t get other stuff done" -- sounds like "we the async devs / end users"
* "when we exit the async with" -- sounds like "current thread of execution"

This ticket is to discuss classes of "we" and what "we" could be changed to.

issue_title: add collection of worked examples to tutorial
issue_body:
We should refactor the tutorial into an initial part that's similar to what we have now, or the middle part of my talk, and then a collection of examples that also serve as excuses to go into more depth on particular topics.

I expect the list will grow over time, but here are some ideas. (Actually the main reason I'm filing this is to have a place collect these so I don't lose them.)

* The current tracing demo should move here. It's a good intro to trio introspection and to co-op concurrency.

* Happy eyeballs

* Multiplexing rpc

* Catch-all exception handler

* Custom nursery, like a race function or ignore-errors nursery (maybe both)

* Some standard stuff like echo server, proxy, fast web spider, ... Whatever doesn't end up in the main tutorial

* trio-asyncio example?

* `nursery.start`

Possibly some of these could be combined or form sequences, eg echo server -> catch all handler -> nursery.start

issue_title: Differing output in /users and /users/:id
issue_body:
So, there's a difference (that I don't quite understand the cause of) in the objects returned by `/users` and `/users/:id`.  Namely, the first don't carry permissions, and have an empty array under the `groups` key.  Meanwhile, the full user objects carry permissions, but have no `groups` key at all.

I'm going to figure out what Ought to be happening and file a bug, but I'm wondering if we need a "re-fetch these when grabbed from a relation at this URI" feature to special-case this stuff. There's a workaround:

```python
aspace.users(list(aspace.users)[2].id)
```

But it's pretty unintuitive; I only found out those objects were different because Bobbi was trying to get permissions earlier today.

issue_title: Make checksumming of downloaded products the default behavior
issue_body:
It is a good practice to verify the integrity of downloaded products and it makes sense to do it by default.
So, let's set `checksum` to `True` in `download()` and `download_all()` and change `--md5` to `--no-md5` in the CLI.
This is a backwards-incompatible change, so we probably want to do implement it in a release that bundles other such changes.

issue_title: Error when generating png style image.
issue_body:
### Error
I get the following error when generating a  png image from text.
```
save() got an unexpected keyword argument 'format'
```
I narrowed down the error to line 91 of maker.py in qr_code/qrcode/
```img.save(stream, format=PNG_FORMAT_NAME.upper())```

The img object has the method save but it does not take in the parameter format.

### Possible solution
Changing the parameter format to `kind` as shown three lines before in line 88. Fixes the problem.
```img.save(stream, kind=PNG_FORMAT_NAME.upper())```

issue_title: Low-Hanging Fruits, Call for Contributions
issue_body:
# Call for Contributions
ONNX is open source, and contributions from the community is welcome. Many tasks / low-hanging fruits, are listed here. If you would like to contribute to the project, but don't know where to start, this list is worth to check.

## Adding ONNX Backend Test Case
Ideally, we should have sample cases for every operator, which can generate [backend test data](https://github.com/onnx/onnx/tree/master/onnx/backend/test/data/) and also serve as [examples](https://github.com/onnx/onnx/blob/master/docs/Operators.md#examples-1). However, many operators have no sample cases, and even not covered by ONNX backend test cases. So please contribute your useful cases to ONNX repository.

[How to contribute ONNX backend test?](https://github.com/onnx/onnx/blob/master/docs/OnnxBackendTest.md)

issue_title: ImageDataGenerator returns empty/white images
issue_body:
Referencing this: https://github.com/keras-team/keras/issues/10869#issuecomment-412351708

I've just been able to reproduce this bug using the following script.

```python
from keras.datasets import mnist

from keras.preprocessing.image import ImageDataGenerator
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import matplotlib.animation as animation
import numpy as np

(X, y), _ = mnist.load_data()
X = X.reshape(X.shape[0], 1, 28, 28)
X = X[:100]


datagen = ImageDataGenerator(width_shift_range=0.2)
datagen.fit(X)


imgs = []
batches = 0
for i in datagen.flow(X, batch_size=32):
    batches += 1
    for x in i:
        img = np.asarray(x).reshape((28, 28))
        imgs.append([plt.imshow(img, cmap='gray', animated=True)])
    print"Completed batch : %i" % (batches)
    if batches >= len(X) / 32:
        break

fig = plt.figure()
ani = animation.ArtistAnimation(fig, imgs, interval=50, blit=True,
                                repeat_delay=1000)
ani.save("test.gif", writer="imagemagick")
```

Which produces the following (blank) gif:

![blankgif](https://i.imgur.com/nOS12d6.gif)

(Apologies about the scripting, did it whilst stood up waiting for a meeting!)

issue_title: [PAPER] Comment on existing libraries/frameworks
issue_body:
There are already implementations of nature-inspired algorithms/frameworks in Python. To name a few, we have [DEAP](https://github.com/DEAP/deap), [Evolopy](https://github.com/7ossam81/EvoloPy), and [Inspyred](https://github.com/aarongarrett/inspyred). It may be worth acknowledging some of these frameworks.

Furthermore, it may also be necessary to expound upon how NiaPy differs from other libraries, does it solve a different problem or use a different software architecture? You can leverage on the algorithms you've implemented that doesn't exist yet in other works, and comment on Niapy in the context of "white-box" or "black-box" approach on software implementation ([ref1](http://vision.gel.ulaval.ca/~cgagne/pubs/deap-gecco-2012.pdf), [ref2](https://github.com/jakobbossek/ecr2#black-box-approach)).
